---
title: "Spatial Transcriptomics"
subtitle: "6 Differential Expression Visualisation"
date: today
date-format: iso
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    theme: litera
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    code-tools: true
    lightbox: true
editor: source
code-block-bg: true
code-block-border-left: "#31BAE9"

params:

  # Project paths and shared folders
  project_root: "."
  # Conservative full-path budget for cross-platform R and graphics-device compatibility.
  portable_path_limit: 200
  dir_outputs: "Outputs"
  dir_src: "src"

  # Differential-expression stage read by this notebook
  de_calc_stage: "5_DE"
  de_calc_results_dir: "results"
  de_calc_results_subdir: "de"
  # Stage 5 tables subdirectory (where contrast_table_expanded.tsv is written).
  # Kept as a Stage-5-scoped param so it does not silently couple to this stage's
  # own out_tables name.
  de_calc_tables: "tables"
  # Metadata column used by Stage 5 to define DE contrast groups. Keep this
  # aligned with 5_Differential_Expression_Calculation.qmd so selected-contrast
  # gene panels can reconstruct the numerator and denominator sample groups.
  contrast_factor: "Annotation"

  # Normalisation-stage objects used for heatmap and gene-level figures.
  # These are read only; this notebook does not refit DE models.
  norm_stage: "3_Norm"
  norm_results_dir: "results"
  norm_rds: "normalised_spatial_data.RDS"
  norm_geodiff_rds: "GeoDiff_normalised_spatial_data.RDS"
  norm_tmm_rds: "TMM_normalised_spatial_data.RDS"
  expression_assay: "normmat"

  # This stage output structure
  out_stage: "6_DE_Vis"
  out_results: "results"
  out_tables: "tables"
  out_figures: "figures"
  de_output_subdir: "de"

  # Differential-expression thresholds used for comparison summaries
  adj_p_cutoff: 0.05
  logFC_cutoff: 0.58            # Approximately 1.5-fold on the linear fold-change scale.

  # Figure controls
  seed: 123
  volcano_point_size: 0.3       # Point size used in all primary and fallback volcano panels.
  fig_width_standard: 10
  fig_height_standard: 6
  fig_width_wide: 12
  fig_height_wide: 7
  fig_width_grid: 18
  fig_height_grid: 10

  # Heatmap typography. Increase these values for denser figures or high-resolution
  # publication output; all values must be finite numbers greater than zero.
  expression_heatmap_base_font_size: 9
  expression_heatmap_label_font_size: 7
  correlation_heatmap_base_font_size: 10
  correlation_heatmap_axis_font_size: 5
  correlation_heatmap_cell_font_size: 2.4

  # Core DE visualisation controls.
  # Core figures always attempt to run from stage 5 result objects and earlier
  # normalised expression objects. If too few genes/samples are available for a
  # method, that individual figure is reported as skipped in the visualisation checklist.
  top_genes_per_volcano: 5

  # Maximum number of top-ranked significant genes drawn in the broad
  # method-family heatmaps. These genes are selected from stage 5 DE result
  # tables and ranked across contrasts by adjusted P-value support and absolute
  # log fold-change.
  core_heatmap_max_genes: 25

  # Selected-method deep-dive controls.
  #
  # selected_de_method must be one of the five stage 5 method result objects:
  #   - "dream_GeoDiffCounts"
  #   - "limma_voom_TMM"
  #   - "limma_voom_GeoDiff" (GeoDiff-filtered count input)
  #   - "limma_trend_Q3"
  #   - "limma_trend_GeoDiff"
  #
  # The default follows the recommended protocol route, but users should confirm
  # this choice using the comparison sections in this notebook.
  selected_de_method: "limma_trend_GeoDiff"

  # Leave blank to use all contrasts available in selected_de_method. Provide
  # exact contrast names only when you want to draw a smaller contrast set chosen in advance.
  selected_contrasts:
    value: []

  # Optional regular expression used to restrict the focused heatmap and
  # segment-filtered boxplots to matching segment labels. Leave blank to use all
  # matched samples and automatic segment inference from contrast names.
  selected_segment_regex: ""
  selected_heatmap_adj_p_cutoff: 0.01

  # Maximum number of top-ranked genes drawn in each focused selected-contrast
  # heatmap. Decrease this value for smaller heatmaps or increase it when a
  # broader contrast-level expression review is needed.
  heatmap_max_genes: 10

  # Number of top-ranked genes to draw per selected contrast. Genes are ranked
  # by adjusted P-value first and absolute log fold-change second.
  top_n_genes: 10

  # Metadata mapping for heatmap and gene-panel annotations.
  # Edit these when adapting the protocol to a dataset with different metadata
  # column names.
  col_slide: "slide_name"
  col_patient: "patient_id"
  col_disease_status: "disease_status"
  col_cell_type: "cell_type"
  col_segment: "segment"
  stain_regex_override: ""

  # Boxplot grouping for the selected-method top-gene panels.
  # x-axis = the cell-type groups the one-vs-rest contrasts compare, so each
  # segment panel keeps >= 2 groups and renders. (disease_status is single-valued
  # within most segments in this dataset, which is why the previous default caused
  # every segment tab to be skipped.) Points are coloured by disease_status so the
  # recovered Tumour/Healthy split stays visible within each cell type.
  boxplot_x_col: "cell_type"
  boxplot_color_col: "disease_status"
  boxplot_facet_col: ""
  facet_ncol: 4

  # Per-segment tab strip in the top-gene boxplot panels.
  #   "auto"  (default): show per-segment tabs only when the segment column is
  #            genuinely distinct from the contrast/grouping axis. If your project
  #            uses one combined annotation column for both cell type and segment
  #            (set col_segment and col_cell_type to that same column), the
  #            redundant per-segment strip is suppressed automatically and only the
  #            "All matched samples" panel is shown.
  #   "always": previous behaviour (always emit the per-segment tab strip).
  #   "never":  only ever show the "All matched samples" panel.
  boxplot_segment_tabs: "auto"
---

<style>
:root {
  --bs-body-font-family: "Segoe UI", Arial, sans-serif;
  --bs-font-sans-serif: "Segoe UI", Arial, sans-serif;
  --bs-font-monospace: "Segoe UI", Arial, sans-serif;
}

html,
body,
body *:not(svg):not(path),
button,
input,
select,
textarea,
caption,
figcaption,
main,
.content,
.quarto-title-block,
.quarto-title,
.quarto-title-meta,
.sidebar,
.toc-active,
.nav-page,
.callout,
.callout-title,
.callout-body,
.cell,
.cell-output,
.cell-output-display,
.table,
table,
table *,
.dataTable,
.dataTable *,
.dataTables_wrapper,
.dataTables_wrapper *,
.html-widget,
.html-widget *,
.sourceCode,
pre,
code,
kbd,
samp,
svg text {
  font-family: "Segoe UI", Arial, sans-serif !important;
}

.cell-output-display,
.dataTables_wrapper {
  max-width: 100%;
  overflow-x: auto;
}

table,
table:not(.dataTable) {
  width: 100% !important;
}

td,
th {
  vertical-align: top;
}

.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable,
.dataTables_wrapper .dataTable {
  width: max-content !important;
  min-width: 100% !important;
}

.dataTables_wrapper th,
.dataTable td,
.dataTables_wrapper td {
  vertical-align: top;
  white-space: nowrap !important;
  overflow-wrap: normal;
  word-break: normal;
  min-width: 10rem;
}

.dataTables_wrapper th {
  font-weight: 600;
}

.dataTables_wrapper .dataTables_scrollBody {
  border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;
}

.protocol-figure-note {
  color: #555;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  margin-top: -0.5rem;
}
</style>

# Differential expression visualisation (6)

::: {.callout-note}
## Protocol reader guidance
This notebook is the visualisation and comparison component of the differential-expression workflow. It reads model outputs generated by `5_Differential_Expression_Calculation.qmd` and produces interpretation figures, cross-method comparison summaries, and selected-method gene-level checks.

It visualises outputs from the protocol-supported DE methods in stage 5 (`limma-trend`, `limma-voom`, and `dream`) without re-fitting any statistical models.

In the recommended protocol path, `limma_trend_GeoDiff` is the default selected method for detailed inspection. This remains a YAML-controlled user decision and should be confirmed using the method inventories, volcano plots, heatmaps, correlation summaries, and gene-level panels in this notebook. Method labels preserve the Stage 5 distinction: `limma_voom_GeoDiff` is the GeoDiff-filtered count-data branch, while `limma_trend_GeoDiff` is the GeoDiff-normalized `normmat` branch.
:::
::: {.callout-important}
## Path portability check
The setup section checks the full input and output path lengths against `portable_path_limit` before analysis begins. Generated diagnostic filenames are shortened in a fixed, repeatable way when needed, and the fixed filenames that later stages must read are never renamed. Fix any path flagged as too long before continuing.
:::

::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
## Reproducibility checkpoint
When adapting this notebook, record all run parameters and decisions, including selected method combinations, thresholds, visual checks, and final model choice, so results remain traceable for publication.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## YAML parameter order guide (6)
To keep this notebook aligned with the rest of the protocol, parameters are grouped in execution order:

1. Project/shared paths.
2. Input locations from earlier stages.
3. This stage output locations.
4. Thresholds and figure controls.
5. Selected-method and selected-contrast controls.
6. Metadata mapping and boxplot grouping.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## How to use `stain_regex_override`
`stain_regex_override` controls the stain or segment pattern passed to `DEResPlot()` when this notebook builds differential-expression summary panels. These panels use the pattern to group or facet DE results by segment identity when that helper is available.

For most datasets, leave this value blank:

```yaml
stain_regex_override: ""
```

When left blank, the notebook reads the observed labels from the configured segment metadata column and builds a regular-expression pattern automatically. Set this value manually only when the automatic pattern is not appropriate. For example, if the metadata contains extra segment labels such as `Background` or `NegativeControl`, but the protocol comparison should only show biological compartments, use:

```yaml
stain_regex_override: "CD45|CD31|PanCK|Stroma"
```

The `|` character means "or" in a regular expression. Match the spelling, capitalization, and punctuation used in your segment metadata column.
:::

::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
## Model choice decision
This notebook provides evidence for model choice but does not automatically select a "best" model. Record the method, contrast set, segment filter, and reasoning you plan to carry into pathway enrichment.
:::

## Load libraries

```{r}
#| output: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(fs)
library(glue)
library(knitr)
library(DT)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(cowplot)
library(pheatmap)
library(NanoStringNCTools)
library(GeomxTools)
library(SummarizedExperiment)
library(Biobase)

set.seed(params$seed)

protocol_font_family <- "Segoe UI"
if (.Platform$OS.type == "windows") {
  try(
    do.call(
      grDevices::windowsFonts,
      stats::setNames(
        list(grDevices::windowsFont(protocol_font_family)),
        protocol_font_family
      )
    ),
    silent = TRUE
  )
}

protocol_pdf_device <- if (isTRUE(capabilities("cairo"))) {
  grDevices::cairo_pdf
} else {
  grDevices::pdf
}

ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_family = protocol_font_family))
```

## Path setup and helper utilities

```{r}
#| label: setup-paths
#| message: false
#| warning: false

resolve_project_root <- function(project_root_param) {
  root <- project_root_param
  if (is.null(root) || length(root) == 0 || is.na(root[[1]])) {
    root <- "."
  }
  root <- trimws(as.character(root[[1]]))

  if (!nzchar(root) || identical(root, ".")) {
    if (requireNamespace("here", quietly = TRUE)) {
      return(fs::path_abs(here::here()))
    }
    return(fs::path_abs("."))
  }

  fs::path_abs(fs::path_expand(root))
}

project_root <- resolve_project_root(params$project_root)
root_via_here <- if (requireNamespace("here", quietly = TRUE)) {
  fs::path_abs(here::here())
} else {
  fs::path_abs(".")
}

path_proj <- function(...) {
  if (identical(project_root, root_via_here) && requireNamespace("here", quietly = TRUE)) {
    return(fs::path_norm(here::here(...)))
  }
  fs::path_norm(fs::path(project_root, ...))
}

dir_src <- path_proj(params$dir_src)
path_utilities_path <- fs::path(dir_src, "pathUtilities.R")
if (!file.exists(path_utilities_path)) {
  stop("Required path helper not found: ", path_utilities_path, call. = FALSE)
}
source(path_utilities_path)
portable_path_limit <- protocol_validate_path_limit(params$portable_path_limit)

de_5a_preferred <- path_proj(
  params$dir_outputs,
  params$de_calc_stage,
  params$de_calc_results_dir,
  params$de_calc_results_subdir
)
de_5a_legacy <- path_proj(
  params$dir_outputs,
  params$de_calc_stage,
  params$de_calc_results_dir,
  "differential_expression"
)
de_5a_dir <- protocol_resolve_input_dir(
  de_5a_preferred,
  de_5a_legacy,
  label = "Stage 5 differential-expression result directory"
)

norm_dir <- path_proj(params$dir_outputs, params$norm_stage, params$norm_results_dir)
norm_input_path <- fs::path(norm_dir, params$norm_rds)
norm_geodiff_input_path <- fs::path(norm_dir, params$norm_geodiff_rds)
norm_tmm_input_path <- fs::path(norm_dir, params$norm_tmm_rds)

out_base <- path_proj(params$dir_outputs, params$out_stage)
out_results <- fs::path(out_base, params$out_results)
out_tables <- fs::path(out_base, params$out_tables)
out_figures <- fs::path(out_base, params$out_figures)
out_results_de <- fs::path(out_results, params$de_output_subdir)
out_figures_de <- fs::path(out_figures, params$de_output_subdir)
out_figures_top_all <- fs::path(out_figures_de, "top", "all")
out_figures_top_segment <- fs::path(out_figures_de, "top", "segment")

stage6_path_audit <- protocol_path_audit(
  c(
    "Stage 5 DE input" = de_5a_dir,
    "Q3 normalized input" = norm_input_path,
    "Stage 6 result directory" = out_results_de,
    "Stage 6 deepest figure path" = fs::path(out_figures_top_segment, "plot_00000000.png")
  ),
  portable_path_limit
)
protocol_assert_audit(stage6_path_audit, portable_path_limit)
knitr::kable(stage6_path_audit, caption = "Stage 6 path-portability check")

purrr::walk(
  c(
    out_results_de,
    out_tables,
    out_figures_de,
    out_figures_top_all,
    out_figures_top_segment
  ),
  fs::dir_create,
  recurse = TRUE
)

message(sprintf("Using project root: %s", project_root))
message(sprintf("Reading stage 5 DE outputs from: %s", de_5a_dir))
message(sprintf("Writing stage 6 visualisation outputs to: %s", out_base))

utility_functions_path <- fs::path(dir_src, "utilityFunctions.R")
if (file.exists(utility_functions_path)) {
  source(utility_functions_path)
} else {
  warning(
    "utilityFunctions.R was not found at: ", utility_functions_path,
    ". Set params$project_root to the project root and params$dir_src to the helper directory name, usually 'src'. ",
    "DEResPlot and plotVolcano fallbacks will be used where needed."
  )
}

volcano_adj_p_cutoff <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(params$adj_p_cutoff))
volcano_logfc_cutoff <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(params$logFC_cutoff))
volcano_point_size <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(params$volcano_point_size))

validate_positive_numeric_param <- function(value, parameter_name) {
  numeric_value <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(value))
  if (length(numeric_value) != 1L || !is.finite(numeric_value) || numeric_value <= 0) {
    stop(paste0("params$", parameter_name, " must be one finite number greater than 0."))
  }
  numeric_value
}

expression_heatmap_base_font_size <- validate_positive_numeric_param(
  params$expression_heatmap_base_font_size,
  "expression_heatmap_base_font_size"
)
expression_heatmap_label_font_size <- validate_positive_numeric_param(
  params$expression_heatmap_label_font_size,
  "expression_heatmap_label_font_size"
)
correlation_heatmap_base_font_size <- validate_positive_numeric_param(params$correlation_heatmap_base_font_size, "correlation_heatmap_base_font_size")
correlation_heatmap_axis_font_size <- validate_positive_numeric_param(params$correlation_heatmap_axis_font_size, "correlation_heatmap_axis_font_size")
correlation_heatmap_cell_font_size <- validate_positive_numeric_param(params$correlation_heatmap_cell_font_size, "correlation_heatmap_cell_font_size")

if (length(volcano_adj_p_cutoff) != 1L || !is.finite(volcano_adj_p_cutoff) ||
    volcano_adj_p_cutoff <= 0 || volcano_adj_p_cutoff >= 1) {
  stop("params$adj_p_cutoff must be one finite number between 0 and 1.")
}
if (length(volcano_logfc_cutoff) != 1L || !is.finite(volcano_logfc_cutoff) ||
    volcano_logfc_cutoff < 0) {
  stop("params$logFC_cutoff must be one finite, non-negative number.")
}
if (length(volcano_point_size) != 1L || !is.finite(volcano_point_size) ||
    volcano_point_size <= 0) {
  stop("params$volcano_point_size must be one finite number greater than 0.")
}

volcano_adj_p_label <- format(volcano_adj_p_cutoff, scientific = FALSE, trim = TRUE)
volcano_logfc_label <- format(volcano_logfc_cutoff, scientific = FALSE, trim = TRUE)
volcano_fold_change_label <- format(
  round(2^volcano_logfc_cutoff, digits = 1),
  scientific = FALSE,
  trim = TRUE
)
volcano_threshold_explanation <- glue::glue(
  "Red points are significantly up-regulated genes and blue points are significantly ",
  "down-regulated genes; both colors identify genes with adjusted P-value < ",
  "{volcano_adj_p_label} and absolute log2 fold change > {volcano_logfc_label} ",
  "(approximately {volcano_fold_change_label}-fold). Black points do not pass both ",
  "criteria. The vertical dashed lines mark the positive and negative log2 fold-change ",
  "cutoffs, and the horizontal dashed line marks the adjusted P-value cutoff."
)

emit_markdown <- function(text) {
  text <- as.character(text)
  if (!stringr::str_detect(text, "\n$")) {
    text <- paste0(text, "\n")
  }
  cat(text)
  invisible(text)
}

.stage6_table_counter <- 0L
.stage6_figure_counter <- 0L

numbered_table_caption <- function(caption) {
  caption <- stringr::str_squish(as.character(caption))
  if (stringr::str_detect(caption, "^Table\\s+\\d+\\.")) {
    return(caption)
  }
  .stage6_table_counter <<- .stage6_table_counter + 1L
  glue::glue("Table {.stage6_table_counter}. {caption}")
}

numbered_figure_caption <- function(caption) {
  caption <- stringr::str_squish(as.character(caption))
  if (stringr::str_detect(caption, "^Figure\\s+\\d+\\.")) {
    return(caption)
  }
  .stage6_figure_counter <<- .stage6_figure_counter + 1L
  glue::glue("Figure {.stage6_figure_counter}. {caption}")
}

protocol_datatable <- function(tbl,
                               caption,
                               page_length = 10,
                               scroll_y = NULL,
                               filter = "none") {
  options <- list(
    scrollX = TRUE,
    pageLength = page_length,
    autoWidth = FALSE,
    scrollCollapse = TRUE,
    dom = "lfrtip",
    columnDefs = list(
      list(className = "dt-left", targets = "_all"),
      list(width = "12rem", targets = "_all")
    )
  )
  if (!is.null(scroll_y)) {
    options$scrollY <- scroll_y
  }

  DT::datatable(
    tbl,
    rownames = FALSE,
    filter = filter,
    class = "compact stripe hover cell-border nowrap",
    options = options,
    caption = numbered_table_caption(caption)
  )
}

first_display_image <- function(output_file) {
  files <- as.character(unname(output_file))
  files <- files[!is.na(files) & nzchar(files)]
  image_files <- files[stringr::str_detect(tolower(files), "\\.(png|jpe?g|svg)$")]
  if (length(image_files) == 0) {
    return(NA_character_)
  }
  image_files[[1]]
}

display_saved_figure <- function(output_file, caption) {
  image_file <- first_display_image(output_file)
  if (is.na(image_file) || !file.exists(image_file)) {
    return(invisible(FALSE))
  }

  image_src <- fs::path_abs(image_file)
  image_src <- gsub("\\\\", "/", image_src)
  caption <- numbered_figure_caption(caption)
  caption <- stringr::str_squish(as.character(caption))
  emit_markdown(glue::glue("\n\n![{caption}](<{image_src}>)\n\n"))
  invisible(TRUE)
}
```

```{r}
#| label: run-parameter-summary
#| results: asis
#| warning: false

run_parameter_summary <- tibble::tribble(
  ~parameter, ~value,
  "Stage 5 source stage", params$de_calc_stage,
  "Stage 6 output stage", params$out_stage,
  "Adjusted P-value cutoff", as.character(params$adj_p_cutoff),
  "Absolute logFC cutoff", as.character(params$logFC_cutoff),
  "Volcano point size", as.character(params$volcano_point_size),
  "DE summary panels", "always attempted",
  "Volcano grids", "always attempted",
  "Heatmaps", "always attempted",
  "Core method-family heatmap max genes", as.character(params$core_heatmap_max_genes),
  "Expression heatmap base font size", as.character(expression_heatmap_base_font_size),
  "Expression heatmap row/column font size", as.character(expression_heatmap_label_font_size),
  "Correlation heatmap base font size", as.character(correlation_heatmap_base_font_size),
  "Correlation heatmap axis font size", as.character(correlation_heatmap_axis_font_size),
  "Correlation heatmap cell font size", as.character(correlation_heatmap_cell_font_size),
  "Method correlation summaries", "always attempted",
  "Venn/UpSet outputs", "always attempted when enough methods have DE genes",
  "Selected method for detailed inspection", params$selected_de_method,
  "Selected contrasts", ifelse(length(unlist(params$selected_contrasts, use.names = FALSE)) > 0, paste(unlist(params$selected_contrasts, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "; "), "<all contrasts in selected method>"),
  "Selected segment regex", ifelse(nzchar(params$selected_segment_regex), params$selected_segment_regex, "<all segments / infer from contrast>"),
  "Selected heatmap adjusted P-value cutoff", as.character(params$selected_heatmap_adj_p_cutoff),
  "Selected heatmap max genes", as.character(params$heatmap_max_genes),
  "Top DE genes plotted per selected contrast", as.character(params$top_n_genes),
  "Top-gene boxplot x-axis column", ifelse(nzchar(params$boxplot_x_col), params$boxplot_x_col, params$col_disease_status),
  "Top-gene boxplot color column", ifelse(nzchar(params$boxplot_color_col), params$boxplot_color_col, params$col_disease_status),
  "Top-gene boxplot facet column", ifelse(nzchar(params$boxplot_facet_col), params$boxplot_facet_col, "<none>"),
  "Top-gene per-segment tabs", ifelse(!is.null(params$boxplot_segment_tabs) && nzchar(trimws(as.character(params$boxplot_segment_tabs))), tolower(trimws(as.character(params$boxplot_segment_tabs))), "auto"),
  "DEResPlot stain regex override", ifelse(nzchar(params$stain_regex_override), params$stain_regex_override, "<auto from segment metadata>")
)

protocol_datatable(
  run_parameter_summary,
  caption = "Stage 6 settings used for this render",
  page_length = nrow(run_parameter_summary)
)
```

## Helper functions

```{r}
#| label: helper-functions

standardise_de_table <- function(tbl) {
  if (is.null(tbl) || !is.data.frame(tbl)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  out <- as.data.frame(tbl)
  if (!"gene" %in% names(out)) {
    out <- tibble::rownames_to_column(out, var = "gene")
  }

  if ("log2FC" %in% names(out) && !"logFC" %in% names(out)) {
    names(out)[names(out) == "log2FC"] <- "logFC"
  }
  if ("adjp" %in% names(out) && !"adj.P.Val" %in% names(out)) {
    names(out)[names(out) == "adjp"] <- "adj.P.Val"
  }
  if ("pvalue" %in% names(out) && !"P.Value" %in% names(out)) {
    names(out)[names(out) == "pvalue"] <- "P.Value"
  }
  if (!"P.Value" %in% names(out) && "adj.P.Val" %in% names(out)) {
    out$P.Value <- out$adj.P.Val
  }

  out %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      gene = as.character(.data$gene),
      logFC = if ("logFC" %in% names(.)) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data$logFC)) else NA_real_,
      adj.P.Val = if ("adj.P.Val" %in% names(.)) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data$adj.P.Val)) else NA_real_,
      P.Value = if ("P.Value" %in% names(.)) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data$P.Value)) else NA_real_
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$gene), nzchar(.data$gene))
}

get_sig_genes <- function(tbl, adj_p_cutoff = params$adj_p_cutoff, logfc_cutoff = params$logFC_cutoff) {
  clean_tbl <- standardise_de_table(tbl)
  if (is.null(clean_tbl) || !"adj.P.Val" %in% names(clean_tbl) || !"logFC" %in% names(clean_tbl)) {
    return(character())
  }

  clean_tbl %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      is.finite(.data$adj.P.Val),
      is.finite(.data$logFC),
      .data$adj.P.Val < adj_p_cutoff,
      abs(.data$logFC) > logfc_cutoff
    ) %>%
    dplyr::pull(.data$gene) %>%
    unique()
}

method_contrast_summary <- function(method_results) {
  purrr::imap_dfr(method_results, function(contrast_list, method_name) {
    purrr::imap_dfr(contrast_list, function(tbl, contrast_name) {
      clean_tbl <- standardise_de_table(tbl)
      if (is.null(clean_tbl)) {
        return(tibble::tibble(
          method_name = method_name,
          contrast = contrast_name,
          n_genes = 0L,
          n_significant = 0L,
          n_up = 0L,
          n_down = 0L,
          min_adj_p = NA_real_,
          has_logFC = FALSE,
          has_adj_p = FALSE
        ))
      }

      sig_tbl <- clean_tbl %>%
        dplyr::filter(
          is.finite(.data$adj.P.Val),
          is.finite(.data$logFC),
          .data$adj.P.Val < params$adj_p_cutoff,
          abs(.data$logFC) > params$logFC_cutoff
        )

      tibble::tibble(
        method_name = method_name,
        contrast = contrast_name,
        n_genes = nrow(clean_tbl),
        n_significant = nrow(sig_tbl),
        n_up = sum(sig_tbl$logFC > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_down = sum(sig_tbl$logFC < 0, na.rm = TRUE),
        min_adj_p = suppressWarnings(min(clean_tbl$adj.P.Val, na.rm = TRUE)),
        has_logFC = any(is.finite(clean_tbl$logFC)),
        has_adj_p = any(is.finite(clean_tbl$adj.P.Val))
      )
    })
  }) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(min_adj_p = dplyr::if_else(is.infinite(.data$min_adj_p), NA_real_, .data$min_adj_p))
}

build_method_metric_df <- function(method_results, value_col) {
  if (is.null(method_results) || length(method_results) == 0) {
    return(data.frame())
  }

  metric_vectors <- list()
  union_genes <- character(0)
  skipped <- character(0)

  for (method_name in names(method_results)) {
    method_tbls <- method_results[[method_name]]
    if (is.null(method_tbls) || length(method_tbls) == 0) {
      next
    }

    for (contrast_name in names(method_tbls)) {
      clean_tbl <- standardise_de_table(method_tbls[[contrast_name]])
      key <- paste(method_name, contrast_name, sep = "__")

      if (is.null(clean_tbl) || !value_col %in% names(clean_tbl)) {
        skipped <- c(skipped, key)
        next
      }

      metric_tbl <- clean_tbl %>%
        dplyr::filter(!duplicated(.data$gene)) %>%
        dplyr::mutate(metric_value = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[value_col]])))

      values <- metric_tbl$metric_value
      names(values) <- metric_tbl$gene

      metric_vectors[[key]] <- values
      union_genes <- union(union_genes, metric_tbl$gene)
    }
  }

  if (length(metric_vectors) == 0 || length(union_genes) == 0) {
    return(data.frame())
  }

  if (length(skipped) > 0) {
    message(
      "Skipped ", length(skipped), " method-contrast table(s) lacking '",
      value_col, "': ", paste(skipped, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }

  metric_df <- as.data.frame(
    lapply(metric_vectors, function(v) {
      out <- rep(NA_real_, length(union_genes))
      names(out) <- union_genes
      out[names(v)] <- v
      out
    }),
    check.names = FALSE
  )

  rownames(metric_df) <- union_genes
  metric_df
}

getFoldChangesDF <- function(method_results) {
  build_method_metric_df(method_results, value_col = "logFC")
}

getPvalsDF <- function(method_results) {
  build_method_metric_df(method_results, value_col = "adj.P.Val")
}

sanitize_for_correlation <- function(df_like, use = "pairwise.complete.obs") {
  if (is.null(df_like) || ncol(df_like) < 2) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  x <- as.data.frame(df_like, check.names = FALSE)
  numeric_cols <- vapply(x, is.numeric, logical(1))
  x <- x[, numeric_cols, drop = FALSE]
  if (ncol(x) < 2) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  min_obs_ok <- vapply(x, function(v) sum(is.finite(v)) >= 2, logical(1))
  x <- x[, min_obs_ok, drop = FALSE]
  if (ncol(x) < 2) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  has_variation <- vapply(
    x,
    function(v) {
      vv <- v[is.finite(v)]
      length(vv) >= 2 && stats::var(vv) > 0
    },
    logical(1)
  )
  x <- x[, has_variation, drop = FALSE]
  if (ncol(x) < 2) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  cor_mat <- suppressWarnings(stats::cor(as.matrix(x), use = use))
  if (is.null(cor_mat) || ncol(cor_mat) < 2) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  finite_cols <- apply(cor_mat, 2, function(v) all(is.finite(v)))
  cor_mat <- cor_mat[finite_cols, finite_cols, drop = FALSE]
  if (ncol(cor_mat) < 2) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  cor_mat
}

format_correlation_labels <- function(cor_mat) {
  label_one <- function(x) {
    parts <- stringr::str_split_fixed(as.character(x), "__", n = 2)
    method_label <- parts[, 1] %>%
      gsub("OneVsRest", "", ., fixed = TRUE) %>%
      stringr::str_replace_all("_", " ")
    contrast_label <- parts[, 2] %>%
      gsub("OneVsRest", "", ., fixed = TRUE) %>%
      stringr::str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
      stringr::str_squish() %>%
      stringr::str_trunc(width = 46, side = "right") %>%
      stringr::str_wrap(width = 18)

    ifelse(
      nzchar(contrast_label),
      paste(method_label, contrast_label, sep = "\n"),
      stringr::str_wrap(method_label, width = 18)
    )
  }

  dimnames(cor_mat) <- lapply(dimnames(cor_mat), function(x) make.unique(label_one(x), sep = "\n"))
  cor_mat
}

plot_correlation_heatmap <- function(df_like, title, out_path, transform_fn = identity) {
  transformed <- transform_fn(df_like)
  cor_mat <- sanitize_for_correlation(transformed, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

  if (is.null(cor_mat)) {
    message("Skipping correlation heatmap ('", title, "'): insufficient finite overlap across methods.")
    return(FALSE)
  }

  cor_mat <- format_correlation_labels(cor_mat)
  p <- suppressWarnings(
    ggcorrplot::ggcorrplot(
      cor_mat,
      hc.order = TRUE,
      type = "full",
      lab = TRUE,
      lab_size = correlation_heatmap_cell_font_size,
      tl.cex = correlation_heatmap_axis_font_size,
      tl.srt = 60,
      outline.color = "white"
    )
  ) +
    ggplot2::labs(title = title) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_family = protocol_font_family, base_size = correlation_heatmap_base_font_size) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = correlation_heatmap_base_font_size + 1, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = correlation_heatmap_base_font_size - 3),
      axis.title.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = correlation_heatmap_base_font_size - 3),
      axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = correlation_heatmap_axis_font_size, angle = 60, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = correlation_heatmap_axis_font_size, lineheight = 0.85),
      legend.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = correlation_heatmap_base_font_size - 1),
      legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = correlation_heatmap_base_font_size - 2),
      legend.key.height = grid::unit(0.35, "cm"),
      plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(8, 14, 28, 28)
    )

  plot_dim <- max(params$fig_width_wide, min(26, ncol(cor_mat) * 0.75))

  ggplot2::ggsave(
    out_path,
    p,
    width = plot_dim,
    height = plot_dim,
    dpi = 300
  )
  out_path
}

sanitize_file_name <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    stringr::str_replace_all("[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]+", "_") %>%
    stringr::str_replace_all("_+", "_") %>%
    stringr::str_replace_all("^_|_$", "")
}

short_file_hash <- function(...) {
  key <- paste(..., collapse = "|")
  key_int <- utf8ToInt(enc2utf8(key))
  if (length(key_int) == 0) {
    return("00000000")
  }

  sprintf("%08d", as.integer(sum((seq_along(key_int) %% 997) * key_int) %% 100000000))
}

bounded_file_stem <- function(prefix, ..., max_chars = 96) {
  key <- paste(..., collapse = "_")
  prefix_clean <- sanitize_file_name(prefix)
  key_clean <- sanitize_file_name(key)
  hash <- short_file_hash(prefix, key)
  reserved <- nchar(prefix_clean) + nchar(hash) + 2
  key_limit <- max(12, max_chars - reserved)
  key_short <- substr(key_clean, 1, key_limit)
  sanitize_file_name(paste(prefix_clean, key_short, hash, sep = "_"))
}

sanitize_col_name <- function(x, default_name) {
  if (is.null(x) || !nzchar(trimws(as.character(x)))) {
    return(default_name)
  }
  trimws(as.character(x))
}

col_slide <- sanitize_col_name(params$col_slide, "slide_name")
col_patient <- sanitize_col_name(params$col_patient, "patient_id")
col_disease <- sanitize_col_name(params$col_disease_status, "disease_status")
col_cell_type <- sanitize_col_name(params$col_cell_type, "cell_type")
col_segment <- sanitize_col_name(params$col_segment, "segment")
contrast_factor <- sanitize_col_name(params$contrast_factor, "Annotation")
model_group_col <- "DE_Group"
contrast_group_col <- "contrast_group"
contrast_source_group_col <- "contrast_source_group"
boxplot_x_col <- sanitize_col_name(params$boxplot_x_col, col_disease)
boxplot_color_col <- sanitize_col_name(params$boxplot_color_col, col_disease)
boxplot_facet_col <- sanitize_col_name(params$boxplot_facet_col, "")

# Per-segment tab-strip mode for the top-gene boxplots: "auto" (default) only emits
# the strip when the segment column is meaningfully distinct from the contrast axis,
# "always" reproduces the previous behaviour, "never" suppresses it entirely.
boxplot_segment_tabs <- local({
  mode <- tolower(sanitize_col_name(params$boxplot_segment_tabs, "auto"))
  if (!mode %in% c("auto", "always", "never")) {
    message("Unrecognised boxplot_segment_tabs value '", params$boxplot_segment_tabs,
            "'; defaulting to 'auto'.")
    mode <- "auto"
  }
  mode
})

metadata_label <- function(x) {
  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(as.character(x), "_", " ")
  stringr::str_to_sentence(stringr::str_squish(x))
}

stage6_discrete_palette <- function(levels, fallback_level = "Unknown") {
  if (exists("protocol_discrete_palette", mode = "function")) {
    return(protocol_discrete_palette(levels, fallback_level = fallback_level))
  }

  clean_levels <- unique(trimws(as.character(levels)))
  clean_levels <- clean_levels[!is.na(clean_levels) & nzchar(clean_levels)]
  if (length(clean_levels) == 0) {
    clean_levels <- fallback_level
  }

  okabe_ito <- tryCatch(
    unname(grDevices::palette.colors(palette = "Okabe-Ito")),
    error = function(e) c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7", "#999999")
  )

  colors <- if (length(clean_levels) <= length(okabe_ito)) {
    okabe_ito[seq_along(clean_levels)]
  } else {
    grDevices::colorRampPalette(okabe_ito)(length(clean_levels))
  }

  stats::setNames(colors, clean_levels)
}

ensure_metadata_column <- function(pd, target_col, candidate_cols, default_value, label_text) {
  if (target_col %in% names(pd)) {
    return(pd)
  }

  candidate_hit <- candidate_cols[candidate_cols %in% names(pd)]
  if (length(candidate_hit) > 0) {
    pd[[target_col]] <- pd[[candidate_hit[1]]]
    warning(
      "Requested ", label_text, " column '", target_col, "' was not found. ",
      "Using fallback column '", candidate_hit[1], "' copied into '", target_col, "'."
    )
    return(pd)
  }

  pd[[target_col]] <- default_value
  warning(
    "Requested ", label_text, " column '", target_col, "' was not found and no fallback column matched. ",
    "Created placeholder column '", target_col, "' for protocol continuity."
  )
  pd
}

# Mirror Stage 5's disease-label normalisation so the Annotation/DE_Group keys
# reconstructed here match the contrast group names written by Stage 5. Without
# this, datasets whose raw disease labels are not already normalised (e.g.
# "Tumour", lowercase "tumor", "normal") fail the contrast-arm match and the
# top-gene boxplots are silently skipped.
normalize_disease_labels <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  x <- stringr::str_trim(x)
  x_low <- stringr::str_to_lower(x)
  dplyr::case_when(
    x_low %in% c("healthy", "normal") ~ "Healthy",
    x_low %in% c("tumour", "tumor", "disease") ~ "Tumor",
    TRUE ~ stringr::str_to_title(x)
  )
}

ensure_de_group_column <- function(pd) {
  if (!(contrast_factor %in% names(pd)) &&
      identical(contrast_factor, "Annotation") &&
      all(c(col_cell_type, col_disease) %in% names(pd))) {
    pd[[contrast_factor]] <- paste(pd[[col_cell_type]], normalize_disease_labels(pd[[col_disease]]), sep = "_")
  }

  if (!(model_group_col %in% names(pd)) && contrast_factor %in% names(pd)) {
    contrast_values <- trimws(as.character(pd[[contrast_factor]]))
    pd[[model_group_col]] <- make.names(contrast_values)
  }

  pd
}

extract_contrast_terms <- function(contrast_expr) {
  contrast_expr <- as.character(contrast_expr)
  if (length(contrast_expr) == 0 || is.na(contrast_expr) || !nzchar(contrast_expr)) {
    return(character())
  }

  terms <- stringr::str_extract_all(contrast_expr, "`[^`]+`|[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_.]*")[[1]]
  terms <- stringr::str_replace_all(terms, "^`|`$", "")
  terms <- terms[!terms %in% c("NA", "NaN", "Inf")]
  unique(make.names(terms[nzchar(terms)]))
}

selected_contrast_inventory_row <- function(contrast_name) {
  if (!exists("selected_contrast_inventory") || is.null(selected_contrast_inventory)) {
    return(tibble::tibble())
  }

  selected_contrast_inventory %>%
    dplyr::filter(.data$contrast == contrast_name) %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 1)
}

resolve_contrast_panel_metadata <- function(contrast_name, pd) {
  pd <- ensure_de_group_column(pd)
  unresolved <- list(
    data = pd[0, , drop = FALSE],
    x_col = boxplot_x_col,
    color_col = boxplot_color_col,
    status = "unresolved_contrast_metadata",
    reason = "Stage 5 contrast metadata could not be resolved. Boxplots are skipped to avoid displaying samples that are not matched to the selected contrast.",
    numerator_label = "",
    denominator_label = "",
    numerator_terms = character(),
    denominator_terms = character()
  )

  if (!(model_group_col %in% names(pd))) {
    return(unresolved)
  }

  contrast_row <- selected_contrast_inventory_row(contrast_name)
  if (nrow(contrast_row) == 0) {
    return(unresolved)
  }

  numerator_label <- as.character(contrast_row$contrast_table_group_1[[1]])
  denominator_label <- as.character(contrast_row$contrast_table_group_2[[1]])
  contrast_expr <- as.character(contrast_row$contrast_table_expr[[1]])

  if (is.na(numerator_label) || !nzchar(numerator_label)) {
    return(unresolved)
  }
  if (is.na(denominator_label) || !nzchar(denominator_label)) {
    denominator_label <- "Comparator"
  }

  numerator_terms <- make.names(numerator_label)
  contrast_terms <- extract_contrast_terms(contrast_expr)
  denominator_terms <- setdiff(contrast_terms, numerator_terms)

  if (length(denominator_terms) == 0 && !is.na(denominator_label) && nzchar(denominator_label)) {
    denominator_terms <- make.names(denominator_label)
  }

  source_group <- as.character(pd[[model_group_col]])
  contrast_group <- rep(NA_character_, length(source_group))
  contrast_group[source_group %in% numerator_terms] <- numerator_label
  contrast_group[source_group %in% denominator_terms] <- denominator_label

  pd[[contrast_source_group_col]] <- source_group
  pd[[contrast_group_col]] <- factor(contrast_group, levels = unique(c(numerator_label, denominator_label)))

  resolved_pd <- pd[!is.na(pd[[contrast_group_col]]), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(resolved_pd) == 0 || dplyr::n_distinct(as.character(resolved_pd[[contrast_group_col]])) < 2) {
    unresolved$data <- resolved_pd
    unresolved$status <- "insufficient_contrast_arms"
    unresolved$reason <- glue::glue(
      "Stage 5 contrast groups were parsed for '{selected_contrast_heatmap_label(contrast_name)}', ",
      "but fewer than two contrast arms were represented in the selected expression metadata. Boxplots are skipped rather than reverting to unfiltered samples."
    )
    return(unresolved)
  }

  list(
    data = resolved_pd,
    x_col = boxplot_x_col,
    color_col = boxplot_color_col,
    status = "resolved",
    reason = glue::glue(
      "Samples were restricted to the Stage 5 contrast arms '{numerator_label}' and '{denominator_label}'. ",
      "Boxes use the configured '{boxplot_x_col}' metadata column and points use the configured '{boxplot_color_col}' metadata column. ",
      "For one-vs-rest contrasts, the denominator arm aggregates the denominator terms recorded in contrast_table_expanded.tsv."
    ),
    numerator_label = numerator_label,
    denominator_label = denominator_label,
    numerator_terms = numerator_terms,
    denominator_terms = denominator_terms
  )
}

get_pheno <- function(obj) {
  if (inherits(obj, "DGEList")) {
    return(as.data.frame(obj$samples))
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "ExpressionSet")) {
    return(Biobase::pData(obj))
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
    return(as.data.frame(SummarizedExperiment::colData(obj)))
  }
  stop("Unsupported object type for metadata extraction: ", paste(class(obj), collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

set_pheno <- function(obj, pd) {
  if (inherits(obj, "DGEList")) {
    obj$samples <- as.data.frame(pd)
    return(obj)
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "ExpressionSet")) {
    Biobase::pData(obj) <- pd
    return(obj)
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
    SummarizedExperiment::colData(obj) <- S4Vectors::DataFrame(pd)
    return(obj)
  }
  stop("Unsupported object type for metadata assignment: ", paste(class(obj), collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

get_expr_mat <- function(obj, elt = params$expression_assay) {
  # Display each method on the scale its DE statistics were computed on, rather
  # than a single global assay (which returned raw counts for TMM and raw exprs
  # for Q3, misrepresenting the modelled signal):
  #   - TMM (DGEList)      -> log-CPM (the limma-voom modelling scale)
  #   - GeoDiff (normmat)  -> normmat assay (already log-normalised)
  #   - Q3 (q_norm)        -> log2(q_norm) (the limma-trend Q3 modelling scale)
  if (inherits(obj, "DGEList")) {
    return(edgeR::cpm(obj, log = TRUE))
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "ExpressionSet")) {
    assays <- Biobase::assayDataElementNames(obj)
    if ("normmat" %in% assays) {
      return(as.matrix(Biobase::assayDataElement(obj, elt = "normmat")))
    }
    if ("q_norm" %in% assays) {
      return(log2(as.matrix(Biobase::assayDataElement(obj, elt = "q_norm"))))
    }
    mat <- tryCatch(Biobase::assayDataElement(obj, elt = elt), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(mat)) {
      return(as.matrix(mat))
    }
    return(Biobase::exprs(obj))
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
    return(as.matrix(SummarizedExperiment::assay(obj)))
  }
  stop("Unsupported object type for expression extraction: ", paste(class(obj), collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

prepare_visualisation_metadata <- function(obj) {
  pd <- get_pheno(obj)
  pd <- ensure_metadata_column(pd, col_slide, c("slide_name", "slide", "Slide"), "slide_1", "slide")
  pd <- ensure_metadata_column(pd, col_patient, c("patient_id", "patient", "PatientID", "Sample ID"), rownames(pd), "patient")
  pd <- ensure_metadata_column(pd, col_disease, c("disease_status", "disease", "condition", "status"), "UnknownDisease", "disease status")
  pd <- ensure_metadata_column(pd, col_cell_type, c("cell_type", "celltype", "annotation", "segment"), "UnknownCellType", "cell type")
  pd <- ensure_metadata_column(pd, col_segment, c("segment", "annotation", "region"), rownames(pd), "segment")
  # Normalise disease labels before building Annotation so the key matches Stage 5.
  pd[[col_disease]] <- normalize_disease_labels(pd[[col_disease]])
  pd$Annotation <- make.names(paste0(pd[[col_cell_type]], "_", pd[[col_disease]]))
  set_pheno(obj, pd)
}

method_expression_object_name <- function(method_name) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    method_name == "limma_voom_TMM" ~ "TMM",
    method_name == "limma_trend_Q3" ~ "Q3",
    TRUE ~ "GeoDiff"
  )
}

is_geodiff_method <- function(method_name) {
  method_name %in% c("dream_GeoDiffCounts", "limma_voom_GeoDiff", "limma_trend_GeoDiff")
}

method_display_label <- function(method_name) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    method_name == "dream_GeoDiffCounts" ~ "Dream",
    method_name == "limma_voom_TMM" ~ "Limma voom: TMM-scaled count data",
    method_name == "limma_voom_GeoDiff" ~ "Limma voom: GeoDiff-filtered count data",
    method_name == "limma_trend_Q3" ~ "Limma trend: Q3 normalised data",
    method_name == "limma_trend_GeoDiff" ~ "Limma trend: GeoDiff-normalized normmat data",
    TRUE ~ method_name
  )
}

make_visual_result <- function(plot = NULL,
                               status = c("generated", "skipped", "failed"),
                               reason = "",
                               output_file = NA_character_,
                               genes_plotted = NA_integer_,
                               samples_plotted = NA_integer_) {
  status <- match.arg(status)
  list(
    plot = plot,
    status = status,
    reason = reason,
    output_file = output_file,
    genes_plotted = genes_plotted,
    samples_plotted = samples_plotted
  )
}

is_renderable_plot <- function(plot) {
  inherits(plot, c("ggplot", "grob", "gTree", "gtable"))
}

visual_result_to_audit <- function(result, method_name, plot_family, expression_object = NA_character_) {
  if (is.null(result)) {
    result <- make_visual_result(status = "failed", reason = "Plot helper returned NULL without a structured result.")
  }

  tibble::tibble(
    method_name = method_name,
    plot_family = plot_family,
    expression_object = expression_object,
    status = result$status,
    reason = result$reason,
    genes_plotted = result$genes_plotted,
    samples_plotted = result$samples_plotted,
    output_file = paste(stats::na.omit(result$output_file), collapse = "; ")
  )
}

display_visual_result <- function(result, title, caption) {
  emit_markdown(glue::glue("\n\n##### {title}\n\n"))

  if (is.null(result) || !identical(result$status, "generated") || is.null(result$plot)) {
    reason <- if (is.null(result) || !nzchar(result$reason)) "No reason was recorded." else result$reason
    emit_markdown(glue::glue("::: {{.callout-note}}\nThis figure was not generated for this render. {reason}\n:::\n\n"))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  if (display_saved_figure(result$output_file, caption)) {
    return(invisible(result))
  }
  caption <- numbered_figure_caption(caption)

  if (inherits(result$plot, "ggplot")) {
    print(result$plot)
  } else if (inherits(result$plot, c("grob", "gTree", "gtable"))) {
    grid::grid.newpage()
    grid::grid.draw(result$plot)
  } else {
    print(result$plot)
  }
  emit_markdown(glue::glue("\n\n<div class=\"protocol-figure-note\">{caption}</div>\n\n"))

  invisible(result)
}

save_grid_plot <- function(grob, file_base, width, height, dpi = 300) {
  pdf_file <- paste0(file_base, ".pdf")
  png_file <- paste0(file_base, ".png")

  grDevices::pdf(pdf_file, width = width, height = height, useDingbats = FALSE)
  grid::grid.newpage()
  grid::grid.draw(grob)
  grDevices::dev.off()

  grDevices::png(png_file, width = width, height = height, units = "in", res = dpi, bg = "white")
  grid::grid.newpage()
  grid::grid.draw(grob)
  grDevices::dev.off()

  c(pdf = pdf_file, png = png_file)
}

sanitize_results_list <- function(results_list, method_name) {
  cleaned <- purrr::imap(results_list, function(tbl, contrast_name) {
    out <- standardise_de_table(tbl)
    if (is.null(out)) {
      message(glue::glue("Skipping {method_name}/{contrast_name}: result table could not be standardised."))
      return(NULL)
    }
    out <- out %>%
      dplyr::filter(is.finite(.data$logFC), is.finite(.data$adj.P.Val))
    if (nrow(out) == 0) {
      message(glue::glue("Skipping {method_name}/{contrast_name}: no finite logFC/adj.P.Val rows."))
      return(NULL)
    }
    out <- as.data.frame(out)
    rownames(out) <- make.unique(out$gene)
    out
  })

  cleaned <- cleaned[!vapply(cleaned, is.null, logical(1))]
  if (length(cleaned) == 0) {
    message(glue::glue("No plottable contrast tables remain for {method_name}."))
  }
  cleaned
}

style_compact_plot <- function(p,
                               title_width = 32,
                               title_size = 8,
                               axis_title_size = 7,
                               axis_text_size = 6) {
  if (!inherits(p, "ggplot")) {
    return(p)
  }

  current_title <- p$labels$title
  if (is.null(current_title)) {
    current_title <- ""
  }

  p +
    labs(title = stringr::str_wrap(as.character(current_title), width = title_width)) +
    theme(
      text = element_text(family = "Segoe UI"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = title_size, face = "bold", lineheight = 0.95),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = axis_title_size),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = axis_title_size),
      axis.text = element_text(size = axis_text_size),
      legend.title = element_text(size = axis_title_size),
      legend.text = element_text(size = axis_text_size),
      plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
    )
}

fallback_volcano_plot <- function(tbl,
                                  title = "",
                                  top_n = params$top_genes_per_volcano,
                                  adj_p_cutoff = volcano_adj_p_cutoff,
                                  logfc_cutoff = volcano_logfc_cutoff,
                                  point_size = volcano_point_size) {
  clean_tbl <- standardise_de_table(tbl)
  if (is.null(clean_tbl) || nrow(clean_tbl) == 0) {
    return(ggplot() + labs(title = paste("No plottable volcano data:", title)))
  }

  clean_tbl <- clean_tbl %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      neg_log10_adj_p = -log10(pmax(.data$adj.P.Val, .Machine$double.xmin)),
      Expression = dplyr::case_when(
        .data$logFC > logfc_cutoff & .data$adj.P.Val < adj_p_cutoff ~ "Up-regulated",
        .data$logFC < -logfc_cutoff & .data$adj.P.Val < adj_p_cutoff ~ "Down-regulated",
        TRUE ~ "Unchanged"
      ),
      Expression = factor(Expression, levels = c("Down-regulated", "Unchanged", "Up-regulated"))
    )

  label_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(
    clean_tbl %>%
      dplyr::filter(.data$Expression == "Up-regulated") %>%
      dplyr::arrange(.data$adj.P.Val, dplyr::desc(abs(.data$logFC))) %>%
      utils::head(top_n),
    clean_tbl %>%
      dplyr::filter(.data$Expression == "Down-regulated") %>%
      dplyr::arrange(.data$adj.P.Val, dplyr::desc(abs(.data$logFC))) %>%
      utils::head(top_n)
  )

  p <- ggplot(clean_tbl, aes(x = .data$logFC, y = .data$neg_log10_adj_p, color = .data$Expression)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = point_size, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_text(data = label_tbl, aes(label = .data$gene), size = 2.5, check_overlap = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = unique(c(-logfc_cutoff, logfc_cutoff)), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(adj_p_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.2) +
    # Okabe-Ito accessible colours for DE direction (blue / grey / vermillion).
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
      "Down-regulated" = unname(protocol_okabe_ito()[["blue"]]),
      "Unchanged"      = unname(protocol_okabe_ito()[["grey"]]),
      "Up-regulated"   = unname(protocol_okabe_ito()[["vermillion"]])
    ), name = "Expression") +
    labs(title = title, x = expression("log"[2]*"FC"), y = expression("-log"[10]*"FDR")) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 8) +
    theme(text = element_text(family = "Segoe UI"))

  p
}

plot_volcano_grid <- function(results_list, method_name) {
  cleaned <- sanitize_results_list(results_list, method_name)
  if (length(cleaned) == 0) {
    return(make_visual_result(
      status = "skipped",
      reason = glue::glue("No plottable contrast tables remained for {method_name}.")
    ))
  }

  volcano_plots <- purrr::imap(cleaned, function(tbl, contrast_name) {
    title <- gsub("^OneVsRest", "", contrast_name)
    plot_obj <- if (exists("plotVolcano", mode = "function")) {
      tryCatch(
        plotVolcano(
          tbl,
          title = title,
          top.genes = params$top_genes_per_volcano,
          point.size = volcano_point_size,
          lab.size = 2.1,
          quadrants = TRUE,
          adj.p.cutoff = volcano_adj_p_cutoff,
          logFC.cutoff = volcano_logfc_cutoff
        ),
        error = function(e) fallback_volcano_plot(
          tbl,
          title = title,
          adj_p_cutoff = volcano_adj_p_cutoff,
          logfc_cutoff = volcano_logfc_cutoff,
          point_size = volcano_point_size
        )
      )
    } else {
      fallback_volcano_plot(
        tbl,
        title = title,
        adj_p_cutoff = volcano_adj_p_cutoff,
        logfc_cutoff = volcano_logfc_cutoff,
        point_size = volcano_point_size
      )
    }
    style_compact_plot(plot_obj, title_width = 28, title_size = 7.5, axis_title_size = 6.5, axis_text_size = 5.5)
  })

  volcano_ncol <- min(3, length(volcano_plots))
  volcano_nrow <- ceiling(length(volcano_plots) / volcano_ncol)
  panel_grid <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = volcano_plots, ncol = volcano_ncol, align = "hv")
  title_panel <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
    cowplot::draw_label(
      glue::glue("{method_display_label(method_name)} volcano plots\nStage 5 method object: {method_name}"),
      fontface = "bold",
      size = 13,
      x = 0,
      hjust = 0
    )
  p <- cowplot::plot_grid(title_panel, panel_grid, ncol = 1, rel_heights = c(0.08, 1))
  out_file <- fs::path(out_figures_de, paste0("region_volcano_plots_", sanitize_file_name(method_name), ".png"))
  cowplot::save_plot(
    plot = p,
    filename = out_file,
    base_height = max(params$fig_height_grid, 3.2 * volcano_nrow + 0.8),
    base_width = max(params$fig_width_wide, 5.2 * volcano_ncol),
    bg = "white"
  )
  make_visual_result(
    plot = p,
    status = "generated",
    output_file = out_file
  )
}

# DEResPlot() can return a ggplot whose faceting/aesthetic problems only surface
# at draw time (e.g. "Faceting variables must have at least one value"), so a plot
# can pass is_renderable_plot() yet still fail later inside ggsave(). This helper
# renders the plot to a throwaway device to confirm it actually draws, so callers
# can fall back to the explicit summary instead of emitting a "not generated" panel.
plot_draws_cleanly <- function(p) {
  if (is.null(p)) return(FALSE)
  prev_dev <- grDevices::dev.cur()
  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  grDevices::png(tmp, width = 200, height = 200)
  ok <- tryCatch({
    if (inherits(p, "ggplot")) print(p) else if (inherits(p, c("gtable", "grob"))) grid::grid.draw(p) else print(p)
    TRUE
  }, error = function(e) FALSE)
  grDevices::dev.off()
  if (!is.null(prev_dev) && prev_dev > 1 && prev_dev %in% grDevices::dev.list()) grDevices::dev.set(prev_dev)
  if (file.exists(tmp)) unlink(tmp)
  ok
}

plot_de_summary_panel <- function(results_list, method_name) {
  cleaned <- sanitize_results_list(results_list, method_name)
  if (length(cleaned) == 0) {
    return(make_visual_result(
      status = "skipped",
      reason = glue::glue("No plottable contrast tables remained for {method_name}.")
    ))
  }

  de_summary_plot <- NULL
  de_summary_table <- NULL
  helper_reason <- ""

  if (exists("DEResPlot", mode = "function")) {
    stains_arg <- if (exists("stain_pattern_for_de_summary") && nzchar(stain_pattern_for_de_summary)) {
      stain_pattern_for_de_summary
    } else {
      NULL
    }

    de_res <- tryCatch(
      DEResPlot(cleaned, foldChangeColumn = "logFC", FDRColumn = "adj.P.Val", stains = stains_arg, title = method_name,
                adj.p.cutoff = params$adj_p_cutoff, logfc.cutoff = params$logFC_cutoff),
      error = function(e) {
        helper_reason <<- conditionMessage(e)
        NULL
      }
    )
    if (!is.null(de_res) && length(de_res) >= 1) {
      de_summary_plot <- de_res[[1]]
      if (length(de_res) >= 2) de_summary_table <- de_res[[2]]
    }
  }

  # Reject a DEResPlot() result that constructs but fails to draw (deferred ggplot
  # faceting errors) so the explicit signed-bar fallback below is used instead.
  if (!is.null(de_summary_plot) && is_renderable_plot(de_summary_plot) && !plot_draws_cleanly(de_summary_plot)) {
    if (!nzchar(helper_reason)) {
      helper_reason <- "DEResPlot() returned a plot that failed to render (for example an empty facet)."
    }
    de_summary_plot <- NULL
  }

  if (is.null(de_summary_plot) || !is_renderable_plot(de_summary_plot)) {
    if (nzchar(helper_reason)) {
      message(glue::glue("Using fallback DE summary plot for {method_name}: DEResPlot() failed with: {helper_reason}"))
    } else if (!exists("DEResPlot", mode = "function")) {
      message(glue::glue("Using fallback DE summary plot for {method_name}: DEResPlot() is not available."))
    } else {
      message(glue::glue("Using fallback DE summary plot for {method_name}: DEResPlot() did not return a renderable plot object."))
    }

    de_summary_table <- purrr::imap_dfr(cleaned, function(tbl, contrast_name) {
      sig_tbl <- tbl %>%
        dplyr::filter(.data$adj.P.Val < params$adj_p_cutoff, abs(.data$logFC) > params$logFC_cutoff)
      tibble::tibble(
        contrast = contrast_name,
        upregulated = sum(sig_tbl$logFC > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
        downregulated = sum(sig_tbl$logFC < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    })

    de_summary_plot <- de_summary_table %>%
      tidyr::pivot_longer(c("upregulated", "downregulated"), names_to = "direction", values_to = "n") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(n_signed = ifelse(.data$direction == "downregulated", -.data$n, .data$n)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(x = .data$contrast, y = .data$n_signed, fill = .data$direction)) +
      geom_col() +
      coord_flip() +
      labs(
        title = glue::glue("Differential-expression summary: {method_name}"),
        x = "Contrast",
        y = "Significant genes",
        fill = "Direction"
      ) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 10)
  }

  if (!is.null(de_summary_table) && is.data.frame(de_summary_table)) {
    readr::write_csv(de_summary_table, fs::path(out_results_de, paste0("de_summary_", sanitize_file_name(method_name), ".csv")))
  }

  de_summary_plot <- style_compact_plot(
    de_summary_plot,
    title_width = 48,
    title_size = 10,
    axis_title_size = 8,
    axis_text_size = 7
  )

  out_file <- fs::path(out_figures_de, paste0("de_summary_", sanitize_file_name(method_name), ".png"))
  ggsave(
    filename = out_file,
    plot = de_summary_plot,
    width = params$fig_width_standard,
    height = params$fig_height_standard,
    dpi = 300,
    bg = "white"
  )
  make_visual_result(
    plot = de_summary_plot,
    status = "generated",
    output_file = out_file
  )
}

select_significant_genes_for_visuals <- function(results_list, max_genes = Inf) {
  gene_tbl <- purrr::imap_dfr(results_list, function(tbl, contrast_name) {
    clean_tbl <- standardise_de_table(tbl)
    if (is.null(clean_tbl)) return(tibble::tibble())
    clean_tbl %>%
      dplyr::filter(
        is.finite(.data$adj.P.Val),
        is.finite(.data$logFC),
        .data$adj.P.Val < params$adj_p_cutoff,
        abs(.data$logFC) > params$logFC_cutoff
      ) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(score = -log10(pmax(.data$adj.P.Val, .Machine$double.xmin)) * abs(.data$logFC))
  })

  if (nrow(gene_tbl) == 0) {
    return(character())
  }

  gene_tbl %>%
    dplyr::group_by(.data$gene) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(score = max(.data$score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(.data$score)) %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = max_genes) %>%
    dplyr::pull(.data$gene)
}

plot_heatmap_for_method <- function(results_list, method_name, expr_obj) {
  genes <- select_significant_genes_for_visuals(results_list, max_genes = params$core_heatmap_max_genes)
  expr_mat <- get_expr_mat(expr_obj)
  genes <- intersect(genes, rownames(expr_mat))

  if (length(genes) < 2 || ncol(expr_mat) < 2) {
    return(make_visual_result(
      status = "skipped",
      reason = glue::glue("Need at least 2 significant genes and 2 samples; found {length(genes)} gene(s) and {ncol(expr_mat)} sample(s)."),
      genes_plotted = length(genes),
      samples_plotted = ncol(expr_mat)
    ))
  }

  heatmap_mat <- expr_mat[genes, , drop = FALSE]
  heatmap_mat[!is.finite(heatmap_mat)] <- NA_real_
  heatmap_mat <- heatmap_mat[rowSums(is.na(heatmap_mat)) == 0, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(heatmap_mat) < 2) {
    return(make_visual_result(
      status = "skipped",
      reason = "Fewer than 2 complete genes remained after removing non-finite values.",
      genes_plotted = nrow(heatmap_mat),
      samples_plotted = ncol(heatmap_mat)
    ))
  }

  pd <- get_pheno(expr_obj)
  sample_ids <- intersect(colnames(heatmap_mat), rownames(pd))
  heatmap_mat <- heatmap_mat[, sample_ids, drop = FALSE]
  if (ncol(heatmap_mat) < 2) {
    return(make_visual_result(
      status = "skipped",
      reason = "Fewer than 2 heatmap samples remained after matching expression columns to metadata rows.",
      genes_plotted = nrow(heatmap_mat),
      samples_plotted = ncol(heatmap_mat)
    ))
  }

  annotation_cols <- unique(c(col_cell_type, col_disease, col_segment, col_patient))
  annotation_cols <- annotation_cols[annotation_cols %in% names(pd)]
  annotation_df <- as.data.frame(pd[, annotation_cols, drop = FALSE])
  annotation_df <- annotation_df[colnames(heatmap_mat), , drop = FALSE]

  # Protocol divergent palette (accessible Okabe-Ito blue -> near-white ->
  # vermillion), replacing pheatmap's default RdYlBu so the DE heatmap matches the
  # volcano and DE summary bars. scale = "row" makes values divergent around 0, so
  # breaks are made symmetric to keep the near-white midpoint pinned at no change.
  heatmap_scaled <- t(scale(t(heatmap_mat)))
  heatmap_absmax <- max(abs(heatmap_scaled[is.finite(heatmap_scaled)]))
  if (!is.finite(heatmap_absmax) || heatmap_absmax == 0) heatmap_absmax <- 1
  heatmap_colors <- grDevices::colorRampPalette(unname(protocol_divergent_palette()))(100)
  heatmap_breaks <- seq(-heatmap_absmax, heatmap_absmax, length.out = length(heatmap_colors) + 1L)

  heatmap_obj <- pheatmap::pheatmap(
    heatmap_mat,
    scale = "row",
    color = heatmap_colors,
    breaks = heatmap_breaks,
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    show_rownames = nrow(heatmap_mat) <= 50,
    show_colnames = FALSE,
    fontsize = expression_heatmap_base_font_size,
    fontsize_row = expression_heatmap_label_font_size,
    fontsize_col = expression_heatmap_label_font_size,
    treeheight_row = 28,
    treeheight_col = 28,
    border_color = NA,
    annotation_col = annotation_df,
    main = glue::glue("Significant DE genes: {method_name}"),
    silent = TRUE
  )
  heatmap_gtable <- heatmap_obj$gtable
  if (is.null(heatmap_gtable) && length(heatmap_obj) >= 4) {
    heatmap_gtable <- heatmap_obj[[4]]
  }
  if (is.null(heatmap_gtable)) {
    return(make_visual_result(
      status = "failed",
      reason = "pheatmap() completed but did not return a drawable gtable object.",
      genes_plotted = nrow(heatmap_mat),
      samples_plotted = ncol(heatmap_mat)
    ))
  }

  out_files <- save_grid_plot(
    grob = heatmap_gtable,
    file_base = fs::path(out_figures_de, paste0("de_heatmap_", sanitize_file_name(method_name))),
    width = params$fig_width_wide,
    height = params$fig_height_wide
  )

  make_visual_result(
    plot = heatmap_gtable,
    status = "generated",
    output_file = out_files,
    genes_plotted = nrow(heatmap_mat),
    samples_plotted = ncol(heatmap_mat)
  )
}

as_character_vector <- function(x) {
  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0) {
    return(character())
  }
  if (is.list(x) && !is.null(x$value)) {
    return(as_character_vector(x$value))
  }
  out <- unlist(x, use.names = FALSE)
  out <- as.character(out)
  out[!is.na(out) & nzchar(out)]
}

limit_file_stub_for_path <- function(file_stub,
                                     out_dir,
                                     extensions = c("pdf", "png"),
                                     path_max = portable_path_limit) {
  extensions <- stringr::str_remove(as.character(extensions), "^\\.")
  longest_extension <- extensions[[which.max(nchar(extensions))]]
  resolved_path <- protocol_output_path(
    out_dir,
    paste0(sanitize_file_name(file_stub), ".", longest_extension),
    path_limit = path_max
  )
  tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(resolved_path))
}

rank_genes_for_plot <- function(candidate_genes, de_table, n_genes) {
  candidate_genes <- unique(as.character(candidate_genes))
  candidate_genes <- candidate_genes[!is.na(candidate_genes) & nzchar(candidate_genes)]

  n_genes <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(n_genes))
  if (length(n_genes) == 0 || is.na(n_genes) || n_genes < 1) {
    stop("Gene-panel plot limits must be positive whole numbers.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  clean_tbl <- standardise_de_table(de_table)
  if (!is.null(clean_tbl) && nrow(clean_tbl) > 0) {
    ranked_genes <- clean_tbl %>%
      dplyr::filter(.data$gene %in% candidate_genes) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        rank_adj_p = dplyr::if_else(is.finite(.data$adj.P.Val), .data$adj.P.Val, Inf),
        rank_abs_logfc = dplyr::if_else(is.finite(.data$logFC), abs(.data$logFC), -Inf)
      ) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(.data$rank_adj_p, dplyr::desc(.data$rank_abs_logfc), .data$gene) %>%
      dplyr::pull(.data$gene)

    remaining_genes <- setdiff(candidate_genes, ranked_genes)
    candidate_genes <- c(ranked_genes, remaining_genes)
  }

  head(candidate_genes, n_genes)
}

left_side_of_contrast <- function(contrast_name) {
  contrast_name %>%
    stringr::str_split("\\s+-\\s+|\\s+-|[-]\\s+", n = 2, simplify = TRUE) %>%
    .[, 1] %>%
    stringr::str_trim()
}

infer_segment_filter <- function(contrast_name, segment_levels, selected_segment_regex = params$selected_segment_regex) {
  selected_segment_regex <- trimws(as.character(selected_segment_regex))
  if (nzchar(selected_segment_regex)) {
    return(list(
      filter = selected_segment_regex,
      status = "user_defined_segment_regex",
      reason = "Used selected_segment_regex from YAML."
    ))
  }

  lhs <- left_side_of_contrast(contrast_name)
  segment_levels <- unique(as.character(segment_levels))
  segment_levels <- segment_levels[!is.na(segment_levels) & nzchar(segment_levels)]

  if (!nzchar(lhs) || length(segment_levels) == 0) {
    return(list(filter = "", status = "unresolved", reason = "No contrast left-hand side or segment metadata levels were available."))
  }

  ordered_levels <- segment_levels[order(nchar(segment_levels), decreasing = TRUE)]
  level_matches <- ordered_levels[purrr::map_lgl(ordered_levels, ~ stringr::str_detect(lhs, stringr::fixed(.x, ignore_case = TRUE)))]
  if (length(level_matches) > 0) {
    return(list(filter = level_matches[[1]], status = "resolved_metadata_level", reason = "Matched a segment metadata level in the contrast left-hand side."))
  }

  prefix <- lhs %>%
    stringr::str_replace("^\\s*`|`\\s*$", "") %>%
    stringr::str_extract("^[A-Za-z0-9]+")

  if (!is.na(prefix) && nzchar(prefix)) {
    return(list(filter = prefix, status = "resolved_prefix", reason = "Used the prefix before punctuation in the contrast left-hand side."))
  }

  list(filter = "", status = "unresolved", reason = "Could not infer a segment token from the contrast left-hand side.")
}

make_plot_summary <- function(source_label,
                              plot_family,
                              contrast_name,
                              scope,
                              file_stub,
                              requested_genes,
                              plotted_genes = 0L,
                              missing_genes = 0L,
                              segment_filter = "",
                              segment_label = "",
                              contrast_label = "",
                              disease_status_levels = "",
                              boxplot_x_column = "",
                              boxplot_color_column = "",
                              boxplot_facet_column = "",
                              samples_plotted = 0L,
                              output_pdf = NA_character_,
                              output_png = NA_character_,
                              status = "skipped",
                              reason = "") {
  tibble::tibble(
    source = source_label,
    plot_family = plot_family,
    contrast = contrast_name,
    scope = scope,
    figure = file_stub,
    requested_genes = requested_genes,
    plotted_genes = plotted_genes,
    missing_genes = missing_genes,
    segment_filter = ifelse(nzchar(segment_filter), segment_filter, "<none>"),
    segment_label = ifelse(nzchar(segment_label), segment_label, "<none>"),
    contrast_label = ifelse(nzchar(contrast_label), contrast_label, "<none>"),
    disease_status_levels = ifelse(nzchar(disease_status_levels), disease_status_levels, "<not evaluated>"),
    boxplot_x_column = ifelse(nzchar(boxplot_x_column), boxplot_x_column, "<not evaluated>"),
    boxplot_color_column = ifelse(nzchar(boxplot_color_column), boxplot_color_column, "<not evaluated>"),
    boxplot_facet_column = ifelse(nzchar(boxplot_facet_column), boxplot_facet_column, "<none>"),
    samples_plotted = samples_plotted,
    output_pdf = output_pdf,
    output_png = output_png,
    status = status,
    reason = reason
  )
}

gene_panel_caption <- function(plot_family,
                               scope,
                               segment_filter = "",
                               segment_label = "",
                               segment_column = "",
                               contrast_name = "",
                               x_col = boxplot_x_col,
                               color_col = boxplot_color_col) {
  scope_text <- if (identical(scope, "segment_filtered") && nzchar(segment_label)) {
    segment_column_text <- if (nzchar(segment_column)) metadata_label(segment_column) else "Segment"
    glue::glue("contrast-matched samples where `{segment_column_text}` is `{segment_label}`")
  } else if (identical(scope, "segment_filtered")) {
    "segment-filtered contrast-matched samples"
  } else {
    "all contrast-matched samples before segment filtering"
  }

  family_text <- dplyr::case_when(
    identical(plot_family, "top_genes") ~ "Top DE expression for this selected contrast",
    TRUE ~ "Requested gene expression"
  )

  contrast_text <- if (nzchar(contrast_name)) {
    glue::glue(" for {selected_de_method}, {selected_contrast_heatmap_label(contrast_name)}")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  glue::glue(
    "{family_text}{contrast_text} using {scope_text}. Boxes compare `{metadata_label(x_col)}` groups across individual samples, and points are colored by `{metadata_label(color_col)}`."
  )
}

plot_gene_panel <- function(genes,
                            panel_title,
                            out_dir,
                            file_stub,
                            source_label = "",
                            plot_family = "",
                            contrast_name = "",
                            scope = "all_samples",
                            segment_filter = "",
                            segment_label = "",
                            segment_column = "",
                            plot_pheno_df = NULL,
                            x_col_override = "",
                            color_col_override = "") {
  requested_file_stub <- file_stub
  file_stub <- limit_file_stub_for_path(file_stub, out_dir)
  if (!identical(requested_file_stub, file_stub)) {
    message(glue::glue("Shortened plot filename for Windows path compatibility: {file_stub}"))
  }

  genes <- unique(as.character(genes))
  genes <- genes[!is.na(genes) & nzchar(genes)]
  present_genes <- intersect(genes, rownames(selected_expr_mat))
  missing_genes <- setdiff(genes, rownames(selected_expr_mat))

  summary_row <- make_plot_summary(
    source_label = source_label,
    plot_family = plot_family,
    contrast_name = contrast_name,
    scope = scope,
    file_stub = file_stub,
    requested_genes = length(genes),
    plotted_genes = length(present_genes),
    missing_genes = length(missing_genes),
    segment_filter = segment_filter,
    segment_label = segment_label,
    contrast_label = if (nzchar(contrast_name)) selected_contrast_heatmap_label(contrast_name) else ""
  )

  missing_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
    source = source_label,
    plot_family = plot_family,
    contrast = contrast_name,
    scope = scope,
    figure = file_stub,
    gene = missing_genes,
    reason = "not present in expression matrix"
  )

  if (length(present_genes) == 0) {
    message(glue::glue("Skipping {file_stub}: no requested genes were present in the expression matrix."))
    summary_row$reason <- "No requested genes were present in the expression matrix."
    return(list(summary = summary_row, missing = missing_tbl))
  }

  plot_mat <- selected_expr_mat[present_genes, , drop = FALSE]
  panel_pheno_df <- if (is.null(plot_pheno_df)) selected_pheno_df else plot_pheno_df

  plot_df <- as.data.frame(t(plot_mat)) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("sample_id") %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = -sample_id,
      names_to = "gene",
      values_to = "expression"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(panel_pheno_df, by = "sample_id")

  if (nzchar(segment_filter)) {
    segment_filter_cols <- if (nzchar(segment_column) && segment_column %in% names(plot_df)) {
      segment_column
    } else {
      unique(c(col_segment, col_cell_type))
    }
    segment_filter_cols <- segment_filter_cols[segment_filter_cols %in% names(plot_df)]
    plot_df <- tryCatch(
      {
        segment_keep <- if (length(segment_filter_cols) == 0) {
          rep(FALSE, nrow(plot_df))
        } else {
          Reduce(
            `|`,
            purrr::map(segment_filter_cols, ~ grepl(segment_filter, plot_df[[.x]], ignore.case = TRUE))
          )
        }
        dplyr::filter(plot_df, segment_keep)
      },
      error = function(e) {
        warning(glue::glue("Segment filter '{segment_filter}' could not be evaluated for {file_stub}: {conditionMessage(e)}."))
        plot_df[0, , drop = FALSE]
      }
    )
  }

  if (nrow(plot_df) == 0) {
    message(glue::glue("Skipping {file_stub}: segment filter removed all samples."))
    summary_row$status <- "skipped_no_samples"
    summary_row$reason <- "Segment filter removed all samples."
    return(list(summary = summary_row, missing = missing_tbl))
  }

  x_col <- if (nzchar(x_col_override) && x_col_override %in% names(plot_df)) {
    x_col_override
  } else if (boxplot_x_col %in% names(plot_df)) {
    boxplot_x_col
  } else {
    col_disease
  }
  color_col <- if (nzchar(color_col_override) && color_col_override %in% names(plot_df)) {
    color_col_override
  } else if (boxplot_color_col %in% names(plot_df)) {
    boxplot_color_col
  } else {
    x_col
  }

  plot_df[[x_col]] <- factor(plot_df[[x_col]])
  plot_df[[color_col]] <- factor(plot_df[[color_col]])

  disease_levels <- if (col_disease %in% names(plot_df)) {
    sort(unique(as.character(stats::na.omit(plot_df[[col_disease]]))))
  } else {
    sort(unique(as.character(stats::na.omit(plot_df[[x_col]]))))
  }
  summary_row$disease_status_levels <- ifelse(length(disease_levels) > 0, paste(disease_levels, collapse = "; "), "<none>")
  summary_row$boxplot_x_column <- x_col
  summary_row$boxplot_color_column <- color_col
  summary_row$boxplot_facet_column <- ifelse(nzchar(boxplot_facet_col) && boxplot_facet_col %in% names(plot_df), boxplot_facet_col, "<none>")
  summary_row$samples_plotted <- dplyr::n_distinct(plot_df$sample_id)

  observed_x_levels <- sort(unique(as.character(stats::na.omit(plot_df[[x_col]]))))
  if (length(observed_x_levels) < 2) {
    # A single-group panel is still informative: it shows the top-gene
    # distributions within this segment. Render it with a brief note instead of
    # skipping, so every segment tab produces a figure.
    single_group_note <- glue::glue(
      "Only one `{metadata_label(x_col)}` group ({paste(observed_x_levels, collapse = '; ')}) is present in this panel, ",
      "so the boxes summarise the top genes within that single group rather than comparing groups."
    )
    emit_markdown(glue::glue("\n\n::: {{.callout-note}}\n{single_group_note}\n:::\n"))
  }

  compact_panel_title <- panel_title %>%
    as.character() %>%
    stringr::str_replace_all("\\s*\\n\\s*", "\n") %>%
    stringr::str_wrap(width = 42)

  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = .data[[x_col]], y = .data$expression)) +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.45, alpha = 0.25, outlier.shape = NA, na.rm = TRUE, color = "#4D4D4D", fill = "#D9D9D9") +
    geom_point(aes(color = .data[[color_col]]), position = ggplot2::position_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0), alpha = 0.7, size = 1.8, na.rm = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~gene, scales = "free_y", ncol = params$facet_ncol) +
    scale_color_manual(values = stage6_discrete_palette(levels(plot_df[[color_col]])), drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = compact_panel_title,
      x = metadata_label(x_col),
      y = "Expression",
      color = metadata_label(color_col)
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_family = protocol_font_family, base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      text = element_text(family = protocol_font_family),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10, lineheight = 0.95),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  if (nzchar(boxplot_facet_col) && boxplot_facet_col %in% names(plot_df)) {
    p <- p + facet_grid(stats::as.formula(paste(boxplot_facet_col, "~ gene")), scales = "free_y")
  }

  pdf_path <- fs::path(out_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".pdf"))
  png_path <- fs::path(out_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".png"))

  ggsave(
    pdf_path,
    p,
    width = params$fig_width_standard,
    height = params$fig_height_standard,
    bg = "white",
    device = protocol_pdf_device
  )
  ggsave(png_path, p, width = params$fig_width_standard, height = params$fig_height_standard, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

  summary_row$output_pdf <- as.character(pdf_path)
  summary_row$output_png <- as.character(png_path)
  summary_row$status <- "plotted"
  summary_row$reason <- "Generated."

  figure_caption <- gene_panel_caption(plot_family, scope, segment_filter, segment_label, segment_column, contrast_name, x_col, color_col)
  display_saved_figure(png_path, figure_caption)
  list(summary = summary_row, missing = missing_tbl)
}

regex_escape <- function(x) {
  gsub("([][{}()+*^$|\\\\?.])", "\\\\\\1", as.character(x))
}

# Decide whether per-segment top-gene tabs would be redundant for a contrast.
# Emily's case: when segment is the contrast axis (or there is no segment column
# distinct from the grouping/x-axis), the per-segment strip just repeats the panel
# or collapses each tab to a single contrast arm. `pd` should be the contrast-matched
# metadata so the "collapses within this contrast" check is meaningful.
segment_axis_is_redundant <- function(segment_column, pd) {
  if (is.null(segment_column) || length(segment_column) != 1 ||
      is.na(segment_column) || !nzchar(segment_column)) {
    return(list(redundant = TRUE,
                reason = "No distinct segment metadata column was available to stratify by."))
  }
  if (identical(segment_column, boxplot_x_col)) {
    return(list(redundant = TRUE,
```
