```{r}
                reason = glue::glue("The segment column ('{segment_column}') is the boxplot x-axis column, so per-segment tabs would duplicate the panel groups.")))
  }
  if (identical(segment_column, contrast_factor)) {
    return(list(redundant = TRUE,
                reason = glue::glue("The segment column ('{segment_column}') is the contrast factor, so segment is already built into the contrasts.")))
  }
  if (segment_column %in% names(pd)) {
    vals <- as.character(pd[[segment_column]])
    vals <- vals[!is.na(vals) & nzchar(trimws(vals))]
    if (dplyr::n_distinct(vals) <= 1) {
      return(list(redundant = TRUE,
                  reason = glue::glue("The segment column ('{segment_column}') has one or fewer levels among this contrast's matched samples, so per-segment tabs add nothing.")))
    }
  }
  list(redundant = FALSE, reason = "")
}

resolve_boxplot_segment_column <- function(pd) {
  candidate_cols <- unique(c(col_segment, col_cell_type))
  candidate_cols <- candidate_cols[candidate_cols %in% names(pd)]
  if (length(candidate_cols) == 0) {
    return(list(column = NA_character_, levels = character()))
  }

  level_tbl <- purrr::map_dfr(candidate_cols, function(candidate_col) {
    values <- as.character(pd[[candidate_col]])
    values <- values[!is.na(values) & nzchar(trimws(values))]
    tibble::tibble(
      column = candidate_col,
      n_levels = dplyr::n_distinct(values),
      n_samples = length(values),
      has_multiple_levels = dplyr::n_distinct(values) > 1,
      values_are_sample_unique = length(values) > 0 && dplyr::n_distinct(values) == length(values)
    )
  }) %>%
    dplyr::filter(.data$n_levels > 0)

  if (nrow(level_tbl) == 0) {
    return(list(column = NA_character_, levels = character()))
  }

  selected_col <- level_tbl %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(.data$has_multiple_levels), .data$values_are_sample_unique, dplyr::desc(.data$column == col_segment), .data$n_levels) %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 1) %>%
    dplyr::pull(.data$column)

  levels <- as.character(pd[[selected_col]])
  levels <- sort(unique(levels[!is.na(levels) & nzchar(trimws(levels))]))

  if (nzchar(params$selected_segment_regex)) {
    levels <- tryCatch(
      levels[stringr::str_detect(levels, stringr::regex(params$selected_segment_regex, ignore_case = TRUE))],
      error = function(e) {
        warning(glue::glue("selected_segment_regex could not be evaluated for top-gene segment tabs: {conditionMessage(e)}. No segment tabs will be generated."))
        character()
      }
    )
  }

  list(column = selected_col, levels = levels)
}

plot_top_gene_segment_panels <- function(genes, contrast_name) {
  segment_resolution <- resolve_boxplot_segment_column(selected_pheno_df)
  contrast_label <- selected_contrast_heatmap_label(contrast_name)
  contrast_panel <- resolve_contrast_panel_metadata(contrast_name, selected_pheno_df)
  panel_pheno_df <- contrast_panel$data
  panel_x_col <- contrast_panel$x_col
  panel_color_col <- contrast_panel$color_col

  if (!identical(contrast_panel$status, "resolved")) {
    skip_status <- if (identical(contrast_panel$status, "insufficient_contrast_arms")) {
      "skipped_one_contrast_arm"
    } else {
      "skipped_unresolved_contrast_metadata"
    }
    summary_row <- make_plot_summary(
      source_label = glue::glue("top_genes:{contrast_name}:contrast_filter"),
      plot_family = "top_genes",
      contrast_name = contrast_name,
      scope = "all_samples",
      file_stub = sanitize_file_name(glue::glue("top_genes_{selected_de_method}_{contrast_name}_skipped")),
      requested_genes = length(unique(genes)),
      contrast_label = contrast_label,
      boxplot_x_column = boxplot_x_col,
      boxplot_color_column = boxplot_color_col,
      status = skip_status,
      reason = contrast_panel$reason
    )
    emit_markdown(glue::glue("\n\n::: {{.callout-note}}\nTop-gene boxplots skipped for this contrast. {contrast_panel$reason}\n:::\n"))
    return(list(summaries = list(summary_row), missing = list()))
  }

  # Decide whether to emit the per-segment tab strip. Under "auto" we suppress it
  # when the segment axis is redundant with the contrast/grouping axis (Emily's case:
  # segment built into the contrasts). "always"/"never" force the prior behaviour.
  seg_redundancy <- segment_axis_is_redundant(segment_resolution$column, panel_pheno_df)
  emit_segment_tabs <- switch(
    boxplot_segment_tabs,
    always = TRUE,
    never  = FALSE,
    auto   = !seg_redundancy$redundant,
    !seg_redundancy$redundant
  )
  no_tabs_reason <- if (identical(boxplot_segment_tabs, "never")) {
    "Per-segment tabs are disabled (`boxplot_segment_tabs: \"never\"`)."
  } else {
    seg_redundancy$reason
  }

  summaries <- list()
  missing <- list()

  if (emit_segment_tabs) {
    emit_markdown("\n\n::: {.panel-tabset}\n\n")
    emit_markdown("\n\n##### All matched samples\n\n")
    emit_markdown(glue::glue(
      "\n\n::: {{.callout-note collapse=\"true\"}}\n",
      "The all matched samples tab uses every sample assigned to the Stage 5 contrast arms for this contrast before any segment-specific subset is applied. ",
      "{contrast_panel$reason}\n",
      ":::\n\n"
    ))
  } else {
    emit_markdown(glue::glue(
      "\n\n::: {{.callout-note}}\n",
      "Showing all matched samples only; per-segment tabs were not generated for this contrast. {no_tabs_reason}\n",
      ":::\n\n"
    ))
  }

  # Bound the stub at source. `contrast_name` is the raw stage-5 contrast expression
  # (~150 chars for one-vs-rest), so an unbounded stub only gets truncated later by
  # protocol_output_path(), which measures the *absolute* path and therefore makes the
  # shipped filename depend on the render root. Bounding here caps the repo-relative
  # path at 124 characters regardless of where the notebook was rendered.
  all_samples_stub <- bounded_file_stem(
    "top_genes",
    selected_de_method,
    contrast_name,
    "all_samples",
    max_chars = 64
  )
  all_samples_result <- plot_gene_panel(
    genes = genes,
    panel_title = glue::glue("Top {length(genes)} DE genes\n{selected_contrast_display_label(contrast_name)} | all matched samples"),
    out_dir = out_figures_top_all,
    file_stub = all_samples_stub,
    source_label = glue::glue("top_genes:{contrast_name}:all_samples"),
    plot_family = "top_genes",
    contrast_name = contrast_name,
    scope = "all_samples",
    segment_filter = "",
    segment_label = "All matched samples",
    segment_column = "",
    plot_pheno_df = panel_pheno_df,
    x_col_override = panel_x_col,
    color_col_override = panel_color_col
  )
  all_samples_result$summary$reason <- stringr::str_squish(glue::glue(
    "All matched samples panel generated{ifelse(emit_segment_tabs, ' before segment-specific tabs', ' (per-segment tabs suppressed)')}. {contrast_panel$reason} {all_samples_result$summary$reason}"
  ))
  summaries <- c(summaries, list(all_samples_result$summary))
  missing <- c(missing, list(all_samples_result$missing))

  # Auto/never suppressed the strip: emit only the all-samples panel and record it.
  if (!emit_segment_tabs) {
    suppressed_row <- make_plot_summary(
      source_label = glue::glue("top_genes:{contrast_name}:segment_tabs"),
      plot_family = "top_genes",
      contrast_name = contrast_name,
      scope = "segment_filtered",
      file_stub = sanitize_file_name(glue::glue("top_genes_{selected_de_method}_{contrast_name}_segment_tabs_suppressed")),
      requested_genes = length(unique(genes)),
      contrast_label = contrast_label,
      boxplot_x_column = boxplot_x_col,
      boxplot_color_column = boxplot_color_col,
      status = "segment_tabs_suppressed",
      reason = no_tabs_reason
    )
    summaries <- c(summaries, list(suppressed_row))
    return(list(summaries = summaries, missing = missing))
  }

  if (length(segment_resolution$levels) == 0 || is.na(segment_resolution$column)) {
    summary_row <- make_plot_summary(
      source_label = glue::glue("top_genes:{contrast_name}:segment_tabs"),
      plot_family = "top_genes",
      contrast_name = contrast_name,
      scope = "segment_filtered",
      file_stub = sanitize_file_name(glue::glue("top_genes_{selected_de_method}_{contrast_name}_skipped")),
      requested_genes = length(unique(genes)),
      segment_filter = ifelse(nzchar(params$selected_segment_regex), params$selected_segment_regex, "<all observed segments>"),
      contrast_label = contrast_label,
      status = "skipped_no_segment_levels",
      reason = "No observed segment or staining levels were available for top-gene segment tabs."
    )
    emit_markdown(glue::glue("\n\n::: {{.callout-note}}\nSegment-specific tabs were skipped. {summary_row$reason}\n:::\n"))
    summaries <- c(summaries, list(summary_row))
    emit_markdown("\n\n:::\n\n")
    return(list(summaries = summaries, missing = missing))
  }

  for (segment_label in segment_resolution$levels) {
    emit_markdown(glue::glue("\n\n##### {segment_label}\n\n"))
    segment_filter <- glue::glue("^{regex_escape(segment_label)}$")
    segment_stub <- bounded_file_stem(
      "top_genes",
      selected_de_method,
      contrast_name,
      segment_label,
      max_chars = 64
    )

    result <- plot_gene_panel(
      genes = genes,
      panel_title = glue::glue("Top {length(genes)} DE genes\n{selected_contrast_display_label(contrast_name)} | {segment_label}"),
      out_dir = out_figures_top_segment,
      file_stub = segment_stub,
      source_label = glue::glue("top_genes:{contrast_name}:{segment_label}"),
      plot_family = "top_genes",
      contrast_name = contrast_name,
      scope = "segment_filtered",
      segment_filter = segment_filter,
      segment_label = segment_label,
      segment_column = segment_resolution$column,
      plot_pheno_df = panel_pheno_df,
      x_col_override = panel_x_col,
      color_col_override = panel_color_col
    )

    result$summary$reason <- stringr::str_squish(glue::glue(
      "Segment tab generated from metadata column '{segment_resolution$column}'. {result$summary$reason}"
    ))
    summaries <- c(summaries, list(result$summary))
    missing <- c(missing, list(result$missing))
  }

  emit_markdown("\n\n:::\n\n")

  list(summaries = summaries, missing = missing)
}

make_selected_contrast_heatmap <- function(contrast_name, de_table, selected_expression_object) {
  selected_heatmap_adj_p_cutoff <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(params$selected_heatmap_adj_p_cutoff))
  if (!is.finite(selected_heatmap_adj_p_cutoff)) {
    return(make_visual_result(status = "failed", reason = "selected_heatmap_adj_p_cutoff must be numeric."))
  }

  selected_heatmap_tbl <- standardise_de_table(de_table)
  if (is.null(selected_heatmap_tbl) || !"adj.P.Val" %in% names(selected_heatmap_tbl)) {
    return(make_visual_result(status = "skipped", reason = glue::glue("Contrast '{contrast_name}' does not contain a usable adjusted P-value column.")))
  }

  heatmap_genes <- selected_heatmap_tbl %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      is.finite(.data$adj.P.Val),
      is.finite(.data$logFC),
      .data$adj.P.Val < selected_heatmap_adj_p_cutoff
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(.data$adj.P.Val, dplyr::desc(abs(.data$logFC))) %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = params$heatmap_max_genes) %>%
    dplyr::pull(.data$gene) %>%
    unique()

  heatmap_matrix <- get_expr_mat(selected_expression_object)
  heatmap_genes <- intersect(heatmap_genes, rownames(heatmap_matrix))
  heatmap_pheno <- get_pheno(selected_expression_object)
  segment_resolution <- infer_segment_filter(
    contrast_name,
    unique(c(heatmap_pheno[[col_segment]], heatmap_pheno[[col_cell_type]]))
  )

  if (nzchar(segment_resolution$filter)) {
    heatmap_filter_cols <- unique(c(col_segment, col_cell_type))
    heatmap_filter_cols <- heatmap_filter_cols[heatmap_filter_cols %in% names(heatmap_pheno)]
    segment_keep <- tryCatch(
      {
        if (length(heatmap_filter_cols) == 0) {
          rep(FALSE, nrow(heatmap_pheno))
        } else {
          Reduce(
            `|`,
            purrr::map(heatmap_filter_cols, ~ grepl(segment_resolution$filter, heatmap_pheno[[.x]], ignore.case = TRUE))
          )
        }
      },
      error = function(e) {
        warning(glue::glue("Selected heatmap segment filter could not be evaluated: {conditionMessage(e)}. Using all samples."))
        rep(TRUE, nrow(heatmap_pheno))
      }
    )
    heatmap_pheno <- heatmap_pheno[segment_keep, , drop = FALSE]
  }

  heatmap_sample_ids <- intersect(rownames(heatmap_pheno), colnames(heatmap_matrix))
  if (length(heatmap_genes) < 2 || length(heatmap_sample_ids) < 2) {
    return(make_visual_result(
      status = "skipped",
      reason = glue::glue(
        "At least two genes and two samples are required after selected-method filtering; found ",
        "{length(heatmap_genes)} gene(s) and {length(heatmap_sample_ids)} sample(s)."
      ),
      genes_plotted = length(heatmap_genes),
      samples_plotted = length(heatmap_sample_ids)
    ))
  }

  heatmap_pheno <- heatmap_pheno[heatmap_sample_ids, , drop = FALSE]
  heatmap_matrix <- heatmap_matrix[heatmap_genes, heatmap_sample_ids, drop = FALSE]
  heatmap_matrix[!is.finite(heatmap_matrix)] <- NA_real_
  heatmap_matrix <- heatmap_matrix[rowSums(is.na(heatmap_matrix)) == 0, , drop = FALSE]

  if (nrow(heatmap_matrix) < 2) {
    return(make_visual_result(
      status = "skipped",
      reason = "Fewer than two complete genes remained after removing non-finite values.",
      genes_plotted = nrow(heatmap_matrix),
      samples_plotted = ncol(heatmap_matrix)
    ))
  }

  annotation_cols <- unique(c(col_cell_type, col_disease, col_segment, col_patient))
  annotation_cols <- annotation_cols[annotation_cols %in% names(heatmap_pheno)]
  annotation_df <- if (length(annotation_cols) > 0) {
    as.data.frame(heatmap_pheno[, annotation_cols, drop = FALSE])
  } else {
    NULL
  }

  # Protocol divergent palette (accessible Okabe-Ito blue -> near-white ->
  # vermillion), replacing pheatmap's default RdYlBu so the DE heatmap matches the
  # volcano and DE summary bars. scale = "row" makes values divergent around 0, so
  # breaks are made symmetric to keep the near-white midpoint pinned at no change.
  heatmap_scaled <- t(scale(t(heatmap_matrix)))
  heatmap_absmax <- max(abs(heatmap_scaled[is.finite(heatmap_scaled)]))
  if (!is.finite(heatmap_absmax) || heatmap_absmax == 0) heatmap_absmax <- 1
  heatmap_colors <- grDevices::colorRampPalette(unname(protocol_divergent_palette()))(100)
  heatmap_breaks <- seq(-heatmap_absmax, heatmap_absmax, length.out = length(heatmap_colors) + 1L)

  heatmap_obj <- pheatmap::pheatmap(
    heatmap_matrix,
    scale = "row",
    color = heatmap_colors,
    breaks = heatmap_breaks,
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    show_rownames = nrow(heatmap_matrix) <= 50,
    show_colnames = FALSE,
    fontsize = expression_heatmap_base_font_size,
    fontsize_row = expression_heatmap_label_font_size,
    fontsize_col = expression_heatmap_label_font_size,
    treeheight_row = 28,
    treeheight_col = 28,
    border_color = NA,
    annotation_col = annotation_df,
    main = glue::glue("{selected_de_method}: {selected_contrast_heatmap_title(contrast_name)}"),
    silent = TRUE
  )

  heatmap_gtable <- heatmap_obj$gtable
  if (is.null(heatmap_gtable) && length(heatmap_obj) >= 4) {
    heatmap_gtable <- heatmap_obj[[4]]
  }
  if (is.null(heatmap_gtable)) {
    return(make_visual_result(
      status = "failed",
      reason = "pheatmap() completed but did not return a drawable gtable object.",
      genes_plotted = nrow(heatmap_matrix),
      samples_plotted = ncol(heatmap_matrix)
    ))
  }

  selected_out_files <- save_grid_plot(
    grob = heatmap_gtable,
    file_base = fs::path(
      out_figures_de,
      bounded_file_stem(
        "selected_contrast_heatmap",
        selected_de_method,
        short_file_hash(selected_de_method, contrast_name),
        max_chars = 72
      )
    ),
    width = params$fig_width_wide,
    height = params$fig_height_wide
  )

  make_visual_result(
    plot = heatmap_gtable,
    status = "generated",
    output_file = selected_out_files,
    genes_plotted = nrow(heatmap_matrix),
    samples_plotted = ncol(heatmap_matrix)
  )
}
```

## Load stage 5 DE outputs

::: {.callout-note}
## Why this section matters
The table below lists the stage 5 result files that this visualisation notebook expects. Missing methods are allowed when the user intentionally ran a smaller method set in stage 5; they are reported so later comparisons remain clear.
:::

```{r}
#| label: load-5a-method-results

method_file_status <- tibble::tribble(
  ~method_name, ~file_name,
  "dream_GeoDiffCounts", "dream_results.RDS",
  "limma_voom_TMM", "limma_voom_TMM_results.RDS",
  "limma_voom_GeoDiff", "limma_voom_GeoDiff_results.RDS",
  "limma_trend_Q3", "limma_trend_Q3_results.RDS",
  "limma_trend_GeoDiff", "limma_trend_GeoDiff_results.RDS"
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    file_path = fs::path(de_5a_dir, .data$file_name),
    file_exists = file.exists(.data$file_path)
  )

readr::write_csv(method_file_status, fs::path(out_tables, "method_availability_from_5a.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  method_file_status,
  caption = "Expected stage 5 method result files and availability for this stage 6 render",
  page_length = 5
)

available_methods <- method_file_status %>% dplyr::filter(.data$file_exists)
if (nrow(available_methods) == 0) {
  stop(
    "No stage 5 DE result files were found. Run 5_Differential_Expression_Calculation.qmd first or check params$project_root and params$de_calc_stage.",
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

method_results <- purrr::set_names(vector("list", nrow(available_methods)), available_methods$method_name)
for (i in seq_len(nrow(available_methods))) {
  method_results[[available_methods$method_name[[i]]]] <- readRDS(available_methods$file_path[[i]])
}

message(sprintf("Loaded %d method result object(s): %s", length(method_results), paste(names(method_results), collapse = ", ")))
```

## Load normalised spatial data

::: {.callout-note}
## What these expression objects are used for
The expression objects loaded here are used only for heatmap and gene-level plots of genes already identified from stage 5 result tables. This section does not estimate model parameters, modify contrasts, or recompute DE statistics.
:::

```{r}
#| label: load-expression-objects-for-visualisation
#| message: false
#| warning: false

expression_file_status <- tibble::tribble(
  ~object_name, ~file_path,
  "Q3", norm_input_path,
  "GeoDiff", norm_geodiff_input_path,
  "TMM", norm_tmm_input_path
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(file_exists = file.exists(.data$file_path))

readr::write_csv(expression_file_status, fs::path(out_tables, "visualisation_expression_object_availability.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  expression_file_status,
  caption = "Normalised expression objects used for stage 6 heatmap and gene-level figures",
  page_length = 5
)

expression_objects <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(expression_file_status))) {
  object_name <- expression_file_status$object_name[[i]]
  object_path <- expression_file_status$file_path[[i]]
  if (!file.exists(object_path)) {
    message(glue::glue("Expression object '{object_name}' was not found at {object_path}; heatmap and gene-level figures requiring it will be skipped."))
    next
  }
  expression_objects[[object_name]] <- suppressPackageStartupMessages(
    suppressMessages(
      prepare_visualisation_metadata(readRDS(object_path))
    )
  )
}

if (length(expression_objects) == 0) {
  message("No normalised expression objects were available. DE summary and volcano plots will still run, but heatmap and gene-level figures will be skipped.")
}

stain_pattern_for_de_summary <- trimws(as.character(if (is.null(params$stain_regex_override)) "" else params$stain_regex_override))
if (!nzchar(stain_pattern_for_de_summary) && length(expression_objects) > 0) {
  observed_segments <- expression_objects %>%
    purrr::map(function(obj) {
      pd <- get_pheno(obj)
      if (!col_segment %in% names(pd)) {
        return(character())
      }
      as.character(stats::na.omit(unique(pd[[col_segment]])))
    }) %>%
    unlist(use.names = FALSE) %>%
    unique()

  observed_segments <- observed_segments[nzchar(observed_segments)]
  if (length(observed_segments) > 0) {
    stain_pattern_for_de_summary <- observed_segments %>%
      stringr::str_replace_all("([.|()\\^{}+$*?\\[\\]\\\\])", "\\\\\\1") %>%
      paste(collapse = "|")
  }
}

message(sprintf(
  "Using stain/segment pattern for DE summary faceting: %s",
  ifelse(nzchar(stain_pattern_for_de_summary), stain_pattern_for_de_summary, "<not available>")
))
```

## Metadata checkpoint

::: {.callout-note}
## Why this section matters
Heatmap and gene-level panels use sample metadata only for annotation and coloring. Review this checkpoint before interpreting visual separation by disease status, segment, patient, or slide.
:::

```{r}
#| label: metadata-column-resolution
#| results: asis
#| warning: false

metadata_checkpoint <- purrr::imap_dfr(expression_objects, function(obj, object_name) {
  pd <- get_pheno(obj)
  tibble::tibble(
    expression_object = object_name,
    n_samples = nrow(pd),
    slide_column = col_slide,
    slide_levels = if (col_slide %in% names(pd)) dplyr::n_distinct(pd[[col_slide]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_integer_,
    patient_column = col_patient,
    patient_levels = if (col_patient %in% names(pd)) dplyr::n_distinct(pd[[col_patient]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_integer_,
    disease_status_column = col_disease,
    disease_status_levels = if (col_disease %in% names(pd)) dplyr::n_distinct(pd[[col_disease]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_integer_,
    cell_type_column = col_cell_type,
    cell_type_levels = if (col_cell_type %in% names(pd)) dplyr::n_distinct(pd[[col_cell_type]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_integer_,
    segment_column = col_segment,
    segment_levels = if (col_segment %in% names(pd)) dplyr::n_distinct(pd[[col_segment]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_integer_
  )
})

if (nrow(metadata_checkpoint) == 0) {
  protocol_datatable(
    tibble::tibble(note = "No expression objects were loaded, so metadata annotation checks could not be produced."),
    caption = "Metadata checkpoint",
    page_length = 1
  )
} else {
protocol_datatable(
    metadata_checkpoint,
    caption = "Metadata columns used for stage 6 heatmap and selected gene-panel annotation",
    page_length = min(10, nrow(metadata_checkpoint)),
    scroll_y = "360px"
  )
}
```

## Method and contrast inventory

```{r}
#| label: method-contrast-inventory

method_inventory <- method_contrast_summary(method_results)

readr::write_csv(method_inventory, fs::path(out_tables, "method_contrast_inventory.csv"))
readr::write_csv(method_inventory, fs::path(out_results_de, "method_contrast_inventory.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  method_inventory,
  caption = "Method/contrast inventory loaded from stage 5 outputs",
  page_length = 15,
  scroll_y = "420px"
)
```

::: {.callout-important collapse="true"}
## Decision checkpoint
Check that the methods and contrasts listed above match your intended stage 5 render. If a method is absent because it was intentionally disabled in stage 5, comparisons below will use the remaining method set.
:::

## DE signal summary

```{r}
#| label: de-signal-summary
#| results: asis
#| warning: false

method_summary <- method_inventory %>%
  dplyr::group_by(.data$method_name) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_contrasts = dplyr::n(),
    total_significant_gene_contrast_hits = sum(.data$n_significant, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_significant_per_contrast = stats::median(.data$n_significant, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_significant_in_one_contrast = max(.data$n_significant, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

readr::write_csv(method_summary, fs::path(out_results_de, "method_signal_summary.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  method_summary,
  caption = "DE signal summary by method using the selected adjusted P-value and logFC thresholds",
  page_length = min(10, nrow(method_summary))
)

signal_plot <- ggplot(method_inventory, aes(x = .data$method_name, y = .data$n_significant, fill = .data$method_name)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.55, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0, alpha = 0.75, size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of significant DE genes by method",
    x = "DE method",
    y = "Significant genes per contrast"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    text = element_text(family = "Segoe UI"),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 9)
  )

signal_plot_file <- fs::path(out_figures_de, "significant_gene_counts_by_method.png")
ggsave(
  filename = signal_plot_file,
  plot = signal_plot,
  width = params$fig_width_standard,
  height = params$fig_height_standard,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

display_saved_figure(
  signal_plot_file,
  "Distribution of significant differentially expressed genes per contrast by method. Wider or more variable distributions should be reviewed alongside the method inventory before choosing results for later interpretation."
)
```

## DE genes for configured contrasts

### Method-family visual summaries

::: {.callout-important collapse="true"}
## How to use these plots
These plots support model comparison and review. A method with visually stronger signal is not automatically "best"; use these figures alongside the method inventory, overlap summaries, correlation heatmaps, and later pathway results.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Comparing differential-expression branches
The result-summary, volcano, and heatmap tabs below share linked tabs. Selecting a method in any group switches the other two groups to the same Stage 5 branch, so you can review the matching evidence together without scrolling back to an earlier set of tabs.
:::

```{r}
#| label: prepare-method-family-visualisations
#| include: false

visualisation_audit <- list()
core_visualisations <- list()
core_de_gene_summaries <- list()

safe_plot_call <- function(expr, label) {
  tryCatch(
    expr,
    error = function(e) {
      message(glue::glue("{label} failed: {conditionMessage(e)}"))
      make_visual_result(status = "failed", reason = conditionMessage(e))
    }
  )
}

for (method_name in names(method_results)) {
  method_label <- method_display_label(method_name)
  message(glue::glue("Generating stage 6 method-family visualisations for {method_name}."))

  de_summary_plot <- safe_plot_call(
    plot_de_summary_panel(method_results[[method_name]], method_name),
    glue::glue("DE summary panel for {method_name}")
  )

  volcano_grid <- safe_plot_call(
    plot_volcano_grid(method_results[[method_name]], method_name),
    glue::glue("Volcano grid for {method_name}")
  )

  method_de_gene_summary <- method_inventory %>%
    # `.env$` is required: method_inventory has a `method_name` column, so a bare
    # right-hand `method_name` resolves to that column under data masking and the
    # predicate becomes col == col (always TRUE), giving every method the full table.
    dplyr::filter(.data$method_name == .env$method_name) %>%
    dplyr::select(
      contrast,
      n_genes,
      n_significant,
      n_up,
      n_down,
      min_adj_p
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(.data$n_significant), .data$contrast)

  expression_object_name <- method_expression_object_name(method_name)
  expression_object <- expression_objects[[expression_object_name]]

  if (is.null(expression_object)) {
    missing_reason <- glue::glue("Expression object '{expression_object_name}' is unavailable.")
    message(glue::glue("Skipping heatmap for {method_name}: {missing_reason}"))
    heatmap_plot <- make_visual_result(status = "skipped", reason = missing_reason)
  } else {
    heatmap_plot <- safe_plot_call(
      plot_heatmap_for_method(method_results[[method_name]], method_name, expression_object),
      glue::glue("Heatmap for {method_name}")
    )
  }

  core_visualisations[[method_name]] <- list(
    de_summary = de_summary_plot,
    volcano = volcano_grid,
    heatmap = heatmap_plot,
    expression_object_name = expression_object_name
  )
  core_de_gene_summaries[[method_name]] <- method_de_gene_summary

  heatmap_audit <- visual_result_to_audit(heatmap_plot, method_name, "Heatmap", expression_object_name) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      heatmap_gene_limit = params$core_heatmap_max_genes,
      heatmap_limit_parameter = "core_heatmap_max_genes",
      ranking_basis = "Maximum -log10(adjusted P-value) multiplied by absolute logFC across significant stage 5 contrasts"
    )

  visualisation_audit[[method_name]] <- dplyr::bind_rows(
    visual_result_to_audit(de_summary_plot, method_name, "DE summary panel", NA_character_),
    visual_result_to_audit(volcano_grid, method_name, "Volcano grid", NA_character_),
    heatmap_audit
  )
}

visualisation_audit_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(visualisation_audit)
readr::write_csv(visualisation_audit_df, fs::path(out_tables, "core_visualisation_audit.csv"))

emit_method_figure_tabset <- function(plot_key, figure_title, caption_fn) {
  # Native Quarto tab grouping: tabs with the same title across every tabset in
  # this group switch together (no custom JS file needed).
  emit_markdown("\n\n::: {.panel-tabset group=\"de-method-comparison\"}\n\n")

  for (method_name in names(method_results)) {
    method_label <- method_display_label(method_name)
    emit_markdown(glue::glue(
      "\n\n#### {method_label}\n\n",
      "Stage 5 method object: `{method_name}`\n\n"
    ))
    display_visual_result(
      core_visualisations[[method_name]][[plot_key]],
      figure_title,
      caption_fn(method_name, method_label)
    )
  }

  emit_markdown("\n\n:::\n\n")
}
```

### Differential-expression direction summaries

Use the signed bar summaries to compare the number and direction of significant genes across the configured contrasts. Large differences between methods should trigger review of normalization and model assumptions.

```{r}
#| label: method-family-result-summaries
#| results: asis
#| message: false
#| warning: false

emit_method_figure_tabset(
  plot_key = "de_summary",
  figure_title = "Results summary",
  caption_fn = function(method_name, method_label) {
    glue::glue("Differential-expression direction summary for {method_label} from stage 5 object {method_name}. Bars show the number of significant genes per contrast and direction using the configured adjusted P-value and logFC thresholds.")
  }
)
```

### Volcano plots

Compare effect size and adjusted statistical support across the same method branches. Interpret the highlighted genes together with the summary bars and expression heatmaps rather than selecting a method from volcano density alone.

```{r}
#| label: method-family-volcano-plots
#| results: asis
#| message: false
#| warning: false

emit_method_figure_tabset(
  plot_key = "volcano",
  figure_title = "Volcano plots",
  caption_fn = function(method_name, method_label) {
    glue::glue("Volcano plot grid for {method_label} from stage 5 object {method_name}. Each panel compares log2 fold-change effect size against adjusted P-value support for one available contrast. {volcano_threshold_explanation} Axes are determined directly from the plotted data.")
  }
)
```

### Method-family heatmaps

Use these row-scaled expression patterns to assess whether each method's top-ranked significant genes form coherent sample-level structure under the configured metadata annotations.

```{r}
#| label: method-family-heatmaps
#| results: asis
#| message: false
#| warning: false

emit_method_figure_tabset(
  plot_key = "heatmap",
  figure_title = "Heatmap of DE genes",
  caption_fn = function(method_name, method_label) {
    glue::glue("Row-scaled heatmap of top-ranked significant DE genes for {method_label} from stage 5 object {method_name}, limited by `core_heatmap_max_genes` ({params$core_heatmap_max_genes}). Genes are ranked across contrasts by adjusted P-value support and absolute effect size. Column annotations use the configured metadata fields.")
  }
)
```

### Per-method DE gene lists

These searchable tables remain separate from the linked figure controls. Use them to check the contrast-level counts underlying the displayed summaries.

```{r}
#| label: method-family-de-gene-lists
#| results: asis
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# Share the native tab group with the figure tabsets so the gene lists switch
# together with the figures. Emit STATIC markdown tables: DT htmlwidgets that are
# print()-ed inside a `results: asis` loop do not emit their JS dependencies, so
# the per-method tables previously rendered blank / not at all.
emit_markdown("\n\n::: {.panel-tabset group=\"de-method-comparison\"}\n\n")

for (method_name in names(method_results)) {
  method_label <- method_display_label(method_name)
  method_de_gene_summary <- core_de_gene_summaries[[method_name]]
  emit_markdown(glue::glue("\n\n#### {method_label}\n\nStage 5 method object: `{method_name}`\n\n"))

  if (is.null(method_de_gene_summary) || nrow(method_de_gene_summary) == 0) {
    emit_markdown("\n\n_No contrast-level DE gene summary was available for this method._\n\n")
  } else {
    emit_markdown(paste0(
      "\n\n",
      paste(
        knitr::kable(
          method_de_gene_summary,
          # Route through numbered_table_caption() so these per-method tables carry
          # a "Table N." number like every other Stage 6 table (the figure tabsets
          # are already numbered per method the same way via numbered_figure_caption).
          caption = numbered_table_caption(glue::glue("DE gene counts by contrast for {method_label}"))
        ),
        collapse = "\n"
      ),
      "\n\n"
    ))
  }
}

emit_markdown("\n\n:::\n\n")
```

### Core visualization checklist

```{r}
#| label: core-visualisation-audit

protocol_datatable(
  visualisation_audit_df,
  caption = "Stage 6 core visualisation checklist",
  page_length = 10
)
```

## Comparing results

### Correlation between methods

These full-matrix correlation heatmaps provide a quick consistency check across differential-expression methods by comparing fold-change and adjusted P-value summaries over shared genes and available contrasts. Hierarchical ordering groups method/contrast outputs that behave similarly. Low agreement does not automatically invalidate a method, but it should trigger a review of model assumptions, contrast definitions, low-power groups, and biological plausibility before later interpretation.

#### Figure legend guide (method-correlation plots)

- **Diagonal values**: self-correlation (= 1.00 by definition).
- **Off-diagonal values**: agreement between method/contrast outputs across shared genes.
- **Row and column labels**: method and contrast labels derived from the stage 5 output names and clustered by correlation similarity.
- **Fold-change panel**: compares `logFC` values. Stable methods and correctly matched contrasts should generally show moderate-to-high positive agreement for related comparisons.
- **Adjusted P-value panel**: compares the untransformed `adj.P.Val` values returned by `getPvalsDF()`. Agreement may be lower than for fold changes; strong disagreements should be documented and investigated.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### User decision after method-correlation review
If cross-method agreement is low, do not interpret a single method in isolation. Re-check contrast definitions, low-power comparison groups, normalization choices, and model settings in stage 5, then re-render this stage before final reporting.
:::

```{r}
#| label: method-correlation-summaries
#| results: asis
#| fig-width: 13
#| fig-height: 10
#| warning: false
if (length(method_results) >= 2) {
  fc_data <- getFoldChangesDF(method_results)
  pval_data <- getPvalsDF(method_results)

  saveRDS(fc_data, fs::path(out_results_de, "fold_changes_all_models.rds"))
  saveRDS(pval_data, fs::path(out_results_de, "pvals_all_models.rds"))

  # Use the safe plotting helper to avoid hclust failures when pairwise
  # correlations contain NA/NaN/Inf because method columns overlap sparsely.
  fc_correlation_file <- plot_correlation_heatmap(
    df_like = fc_data,
    title = "Fold-change correlation",
    out_path = fs::path(out_figures_de, "fc_correlation_methods.png")
  )
  display_saved_figure(
    fc_correlation_file,
    "Full correlation heatmap of logFC summaries across method and contrast outputs. Hierarchically grouped off-diagonal agreement supports consistent effect-size direction across methods, while weak blocks should trigger review of model assumptions and contrast definitions."
  )

  pval_correlation_file <- plot_correlation_heatmap(
    df_like = pval_data,
    title = "P-value correlation",
    out_path = fs::path(out_figures_de, "pval_correlation_methods.png")
  )
  display_saved_figure(
    pval_correlation_file,
    "Full correlation heatmap of untransformed adjusted P-value summaries across method and contrast outputs. Divergent off-diagonal structure indicates that adjusted statistical support differs by method and should be documented before later interpretation."
  )
}
```

### Conserved genes across active methods

This summary reports genes that pass the configured adjusted P-value and absolute logFC thresholds in the same contrast across every active method. These conservative, cross-method sets provide a high-confidence review list; users should still inspect effect direction, expression patterns, and model suitability before later interpretation.

```{r}
#| label: conserved-genes-across-methods
#| warning: false
if (length(method_results) >= 2) {
  contrast_names <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(method_results, names))

  # Use the same standardised significant-gene extraction as the overlap block
  # below, so gene identity (standardised `gene` column) and NA handling are
  # consistent across the two cross-method summaries.
  genes_by_model_contrast <- lapply(method_results, function(model_list) {
    lapply(model_list[contrast_names], function(tbl) {
      get_sig_genes(tbl)
    })
  })

  conserved <- lapply(contrast_names, function(ct) {
    sets <- lapply(genes_by_model_contrast, function(model_contrasts) model_contrasts[[ct]])
    Reduce(intersect, sets)
  })
  names(conserved) <- contrast_names

  conserved_df <- tibble::tibble(
    contrast = names(conserved),
    n_conserved = lengths(conserved),
    genes = vapply(conserved, function(x) paste(x, collapse = ";"), character(1))
  )

  readr::write_csv(conserved_df, fs::path(out_results_de, "conserved_genes_across_models.csv"))
  saveRDS(conserved, fs::path(out_results_de, "conserved_genes_across_models.rds"))
}
```

### DE gene overlap across methods

```{r}
#| label: de-gene-overlap
#| results: asis
#| warning: false

de_genes_by_method <- purrr::map(method_results, function(contrast_list) {
  unique(unlist(purrr::map(contrast_list, get_sig_genes), use.names = FALSE))
}) %>%
  purrr::keep(~ length(.x) > 0)

de_gene_count_table <- tibble::tibble(
  method_name = names(de_genes_by_method),
  n_unique_significant_genes = purrr::map_int(de_genes_by_method, length)
)

readr::write_csv(de_gene_count_table, fs::path(out_results_de, "de_gene_counts_by_method.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  de_gene_count_table,
  caption = "Unique significant DE genes by method across all available contrasts",
  page_length = min(10, max(1, nrow(de_gene_count_table)))
)

if (length(de_genes_by_method) > 0) {
  robust_DE_genes <- Reduce(intersect, de_genes_by_method)
} else {
  robust_DE_genes <- character()
}

writeLines(robust_DE_genes, fs::path(out_results_de, "robust_DE_genes.txt"))

overlap_count_plot_df <- de_gene_count_table %>%
  dplyr::arrange(.data$n_unique_significant_genes) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method_name = factor(.data$method_name, levels = .data$method_name))

overlap_count_plot <- ggplot(
  overlap_count_plot_df,
  aes(x = .data$n_unique_significant_genes, y = .data$method_name, fill = .data$method_name)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.66) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = format(.data$n_unique_significant_genes, big.mark = ",")),
    hjust = -0.18, size = 3.3, colour = "#333333", family = protocol_font_family
  ) +
  # Categorical (DE method) -> accessible Okabe-Ito palette.
  scale_fill_manual(values = stage6_discrete_palette(levels(overlap_count_plot_df$method_name)), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0, 0.14)),
    labels = function(x) format(x, big.mark = ",")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Unique significant DE genes by method",
    subtitle = glue::glue("Genes with adj. P < {params$adj_p_cutoff} and |log2FC| > {params$logFC_cutoff} in any available contrast"),
    x = "Unique significant genes",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11, base_family = protocol_font_family) +
  theme(
    text = element_text(family = protocol_font_family),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, colour = "#555555", margin = ggplot2::margin(b = 8)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8.5),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3, colour = "#ECECEC"),
    plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 8, r = 16, b = 8, l = 8)
  )

overlap_count_file <- fs::path(out_figures_de, "de_gene_counts_by_method.png")
ggsave(
  filename = overlap_count_file,
  plot = overlap_count_plot,
  width = params$fig_width_standard,
  height = params$fig_height_standard,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

display_saved_figure(
  overlap_count_file,
  "Bar plot of unique significant differentially expressed genes recovered by each method across all available contrasts. Large differences in count should be interpreted with the correlation and overlap diagnostics before method selection."
)
```

### Venn and UpSet plots

```{r}
#| label: venn-upset-overlap
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| results: asis

if (length(de_genes_by_method) >= 2) {
  if (length(de_genes_by_method) <= 5 && requireNamespace("VennDiagram", quietly = TRUE)) {
    venn_file <- fs::path(out_figures_de, "DE_genes_model_comparisons.png")
    suppressMessages(
      suppressWarnings(
        VennDiagram::venn.diagram(
          x = de_genes_by_method,
          imagetype = "png",
          filename = venn_file,
          output = TRUE,
          height = 1800,
          width = 2200,
          resolution = 300,
          compression = "lzw",
          lwd = 1,
          cex = 0.8,
          cat.cex = 0.7,
          margin = 0.08
        )
      )
    )
    display_saved_figure(
      venn_file,
      "Venn diagram of significant differentially expressed gene overlap across methods. Shared regions represent genes detected by multiple methods and support prioritization of DE signals that recur across methods."
    )
  } else {
    message("Skipping Venn diagram: VennDiagram is unavailable or more than five methods have DE genes.")
  }

  if (requireNamespace("ComplexHeatmap", quietly = TRUE)) {
    upset_plot <- ComplexHeatmap::UpSet(ComplexHeatmap::make_comb_mat(de_genes_by_method))
    upset_pdf <- fs::path(out_figures_de, "DE_genes_model_upset.pdf")
    upset_png <- fs::path(out_figures_de, "DE_genes_model_upset.png")

    grDevices::pdf(upset_pdf, width = 9, height = 6)
    ComplexHeatmap::draw(upset_plot)
    invisible(grDevices::dev.off())

    grDevices::png(upset_png, width = 9, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300, bg = "white")
    ComplexHeatmap::draw(upset_plot)
    invisible(grDevices::dev.off())

    display_saved_figure(
      upset_png,
      "UpSet plot of significant differentially expressed gene intersections across methods. The matrix view is used when overlap structure is too complex for a compact Venn diagram."
    )
  } else {
    message("Skipping UpSet plot: ComplexHeatmap is not available.")
  }
} else {
  message("Skipping Venn/UpSet outputs: fewer than two methods have significant DE genes.")
}
```

## Selected method and selected contrast deep dive

::: {.callout-important}
## Required user decision
Use `selected_de_method` and, optionally, `selected_contrasts` and `selected_segment_regex` to choose the detailed gene-level inspection shown below. The default selected method is `limma_trend_GeoDiff`, but the comparison evidence above should be reviewed before treating this as the final analysis route.
:::

```{r}
#| label: selected-method-contrast-inventory
#| results: asis
#| warning: false

selected_de_method <- trimws(as.character(params$selected_de_method))
requested_selected_contrasts <- as_character_vector(params$selected_contrasts)
selected_method_available <- selected_de_method %in% names(method_results)
selected_results <- if (selected_method_available) method_results[[selected_de_method]] else list()
available_selected_contrasts <- names(selected_results)

if (is.null(available_selected_contrasts)) {
  available_selected_contrasts <- character()
}

if (length(requested_selected_contrasts) > 0) {
  missing_requested_contrasts <- setdiff(requested_selected_contrasts, available_selected_contrasts)
  selected_contrasts <- intersect(requested_selected_contrasts, available_selected_contrasts)
} else {
  missing_requested_contrasts <- character()
  selected_contrasts <- available_selected_contrasts
}

selected_deep_dive_ready <- selected_method_available && length(selected_contrasts) > 0

selected_method_status <- tibble::tibble(
  selected_de_method = selected_de_method,
  method_available = selected_method_available,
  requested_contrasts = ifelse(length(requested_selected_contrasts) > 0, paste(requested_selected_contrasts, collapse = "; "), "<all contrasts in selected method>"),
  contrasts_available_in_method = length(available_selected_contrasts),
  contrasts_used = length(selected_contrasts),
  missing_requested_contrasts = ifelse(length(missing_requested_contrasts) > 0, paste(missing_requested_contrasts, collapse = "; "), "<none>"),
  status = dplyr::case_when(
    !selected_method_available & is_geodiff_method(selected_de_method) ~ "skipped_missing_geodiff_or_dream_method",
    !selected_method_available ~ "skipped_missing_selected_method",
    length(selected_contrasts) == 0 ~ "skipped_no_selected_contrasts",
    TRUE ~ "ready"
  ),
  action = dplyr::case_when(
    selected_method_available & length(selected_contrasts) > 0 ~ "Proceed with selected-method figures.",
    is_geodiff_method(selected_de_method) ~ "Selected method was not loaded. If GeoDiff or dream was intentionally skipped or failed in an earlier stage, select an available non-GeoDiff method such as limma_trend_Q3 or limma_voom_TMM.",
    TRUE ~ "Select one of the available methods listed in the method inventory."
  )
)

readr::write_csv(selected_method_status, fs::path(out_tables, "selected_method_status.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  selected_method_status,
  caption = "Selected-method configuration and availability",
  page_length = 1,
  scroll_y = "220px"
)

expanded_contrast_path <- path_proj(params$dir_outputs, params$de_calc_stage, params$de_calc_tables, "contrast_table_expanded.tsv")
expanded_contrast_table <- if (file.exists(expanded_contrast_path)) {
  readr::read_tsv(expanded_contrast_path, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      group_1 = as.character(.data$group_1),
      group_2 = as.character(.data$group_2),
      contrast_expr = as.character(.data$contrast_expr),
      contrast_name_group_style = paste0(.data$group_1, "_vs_", .data$group_2)
    )
} else {
  tibble::tibble(
    group_1 = character(),
    group_2 = character(),
    contrast_expr = character(),
    contrast_name_group_style = character()
  )
}

contrast_expr_lookup <- expanded_contrast_table %>%
  dplyr::select(
    contrast = contrast_expr,
    contrast_table_group_1 = group_1,
    contrast_table_group_2 = group_2,
    contrast_table_expr = contrast_expr
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(contrast_table_match_status = "exact_contrast_expression")

contrast_group_lookup <- expanded_contrast_table %>%
  dplyr::select(
    contrast = contrast_name_group_style,
    contrast_table_group_1 = group_1,
    contrast_table_group_2 = group_2,
    contrast_table_expr = contrast_expr
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(contrast_table_match_status = "group_1_vs_group_2_name")

contrast_table_lookup <- dplyr::bind_rows(contrast_expr_lookup, contrast_group_lookup) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$contrast), nzchar(.data$contrast)) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(contrast, .keep_all = TRUE)

selected_contrast_inventory <- tibble::tibble(
  method_name = selected_de_method,
  contrast = selected_contrasts,
  n_genes = purrr::map_int(selected_results[selected_contrasts], nrow)
) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    method_inventory %>%
      dplyr::mutate(found_in_stage5_inventory = TRUE) %>%
      dplyr::select(
        method_name,
        contrast,
        n_genes_tested_stage5 = n_genes,
        n_significant,
        n_up,
        n_down,
        found_in_stage5_inventory
      ),
    by = c("method_name", "contrast")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(contrast_table_lookup, by = "contrast") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    contrast_panel = sprintf("Contrast %02d", dplyr::row_number()),
    contrast_readable_label = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(.data$contrast_table_group_1) &
        nzchar(.data$contrast_table_group_1) &
        !is.na(.data$contrast_table_group_2) &
        nzchar(.data$contrast_table_group_2) ~ stringr::str_squish(paste(.data$contrast_table_group_1, "vs", .data$contrast_table_group_2)),
      TRUE ~ as.character(.data$contrast)
    ),
    contrast_heatmap_label = stringr::str_squish(paste(.data$contrast_panel, .data$contrast_readable_label, sep = ": ")),
    found_in_stage5_inventory = dplyr::coalesce(.data$found_in_stage5_inventory, FALSE),
    contrast_table_match_status = dplyr::case_when(
      !file.exists(expanded_contrast_path) ~ "contrast_table_expanded.tsv not found",
      is.na(.data$contrast_table_match_status) ~ "not_matched_to_expanded_contrast_table",
      TRUE ~ .data$contrast_table_match_status
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::relocate(contrast_panel, .after = method_name)

selected_contrast_label_lookup <- stats::setNames(selected_contrast_inventory$contrast_panel, selected_contrast_inventory$contrast)
selected_contrast_display_label <- function(contrast_name) {
  label <- unname(selected_contrast_label_lookup[contrast_name])
  if (length(label) == 0 || is.na(label) || !nzchar(label)) {
    return("Contrast panel")
  }
  label
}

selected_contrast_heatmap_label_lookup <- stats::setNames(selected_contrast_inventory$contrast_heatmap_label, selected_contrast_inventory$contrast)
selected_contrast_heatmap_label <- function(contrast_name) {
  label <- unname(selected_contrast_heatmap_label_lookup[contrast_name])
  if (length(label) == 0 || is.na(label) || !nzchar(label)) {
    return(stringr::str_squish(paste(selected_contrast_display_label(contrast_name), contrast_name, sep = ": ")))
  }
  label
}

selected_contrast_heatmap_title <- function(contrast_name, width = 72) {
  stringr::str_wrap(selected_contrast_heatmap_label(contrast_name), width = width)
}

readr::write_csv(selected_contrast_inventory, fs::path(out_tables, "selected_method_contrast_inventory.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  selected_contrast_inventory,
  caption = "Contrasts used for selected-method deep inspection",
  page_length = 15,
  scroll_y = "420px"
)
```

## Selected expression metadata checkpoint

::: {.callout-note}
## What to check
Confirm that the expression object matches the selected method and that the metadata columns used for grouping, color, faceting, and segment filtering are present before interpreting selected-method boxplots.
:::

```{r}
#| label: selected-expression-metadata
#| results: asis
#| warning: false

selected_expression_object_name <- if (selected_method_available) method_expression_object_name(selected_de_method) else NA_character_
selected_expression_object <- if (!is.na(selected_expression_object_name)) expression_objects[[selected_expression_object_name]] else NULL
selected_expression_ready <- selected_deep_dive_ready && !is.null(selected_expression_object)

selected_expr_mat <- matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = 0)
selected_pheno_df <- tibble::tibble(sample_id = character())

if (selected_expression_ready) {
  selected_expr_mat <- get_expr_mat(selected_expression_object)
  selected_pheno_df <- get_pheno(selected_expression_object)
  selected_pheno_df$sample_id <- rownames(selected_pheno_df)

  selected_pheno_df <- ensure_metadata_column(selected_pheno_df, boxplot_x_col, c(col_disease, col_cell_type, col_segment), "UnknownGroup", "boxplot x-axis group")
  selected_pheno_df <- ensure_metadata_column(selected_pheno_df, boxplot_color_col, c(col_segment, col_disease, col_cell_type), "UnknownGroup", "boxplot color group")
  if (nzchar(boxplot_facet_col)) {
    selected_pheno_df <- ensure_metadata_column(selected_pheno_df, boxplot_facet_col, c(col_cell_type, col_segment), "UnknownGroup", "optional boxplot facet")
  }

  shared_samples <- intersect(colnames(selected_expr_mat), selected_pheno_df$sample_id)
  if (length(shared_samples) < 2) {
    selected_expression_ready <- FALSE
    selected_expression_reason <- "Fewer than two samples overlap between the selected expression matrix and metadata."
  } else {
    selected_expr_mat <- selected_expr_mat[, shared_samples, drop = FALSE]
    selected_pheno_df <- selected_pheno_df %>%
      dplyr::filter(.data$sample_id %in% shared_samples)
    selected_expression_reason <- "Ready."
  }
} else {
  selected_expression_reason <- if (selected_deep_dive_ready) {
    glue::glue("Expression object '{selected_expression_object_name}' is unavailable.")
  } else {
    "Selected method or selected contrasts are not available."
  }
}

selected_expression_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  selected_method = selected_de_method,
  expression_object = ifelse(is.na(selected_expression_object_name), "<not resolved>", selected_expression_object_name),
  status = ifelse(selected_expression_ready, "ready", "skipped"),
  reason = selected_expression_reason,
  boxplot_x_column = boxplot_x_col,
  boxplot_color_column = boxplot_color_col,
  boxplot_facet_column = ifelse(nzchar(boxplot_facet_col), boxplot_facet_col, "<none>"),
  genes = nrow(selected_expr_mat),
  samples = ncol(selected_expr_mat)
)

readr::write_csv(selected_expression_summary, fs::path(out_tables, "selected_expression_object_summary.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  selected_expression_summary,
  caption = "Expression object used for selected-method gene-level figures",
  page_length = 1
)

segment_label_audit <- if (selected_expression_ready && col_segment %in% names(selected_pheno_df)) {
  selected_pheno_df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      segment_label = as.character(.data[[col_segment]]),
      missing_or_blank_segment = is.na(.data$segment_label) | !nzchar(trimws(.data$segment_label))
    ) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      selected_method = selected_de_method,
      segment_column = col_segment,
      samples_checked = dplyr::n(),
      samples_with_missing_or_blank_segment = sum(.data$missing_or_blank_segment),
      segment_levels = paste(sort(unique(stats::na.omit(.data$segment_label))), collapse = "; "),
      status = ifelse(samples_with_missing_or_blank_segment > 0, "review_required", "passed"),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
} else {
  tibble::tibble(
    selected_method = selected_de_method,
    segment_column = col_segment,
    samples_checked = 0L,
    samples_with_missing_or_blank_segment = NA_integer_,
    segment_levels = "",
    status = "skipped",
    reason = "Selected expression metadata was unavailable."
  )
}

readr::write_csv(segment_label_audit, fs::path(out_tables, "segment_label_audit.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  segment_label_audit,
  caption = "Selected-method segment label check",
  page_length = 5
)
```

## Focused selected-contrast heatmaps

::: {.callout-note}
## What this section shows
These heatmaps focus on the selected method and selected contrast set. They use the configured adjusted P-value threshold (`selected_heatmap_adj_p_cutoff`) and selected-contrast gene limit (`heatmap_max_genes`) so the output remains compact enough for interpretation.
:::

```{r}
#| label: selected-focused-heatmaps
#| results: asis
#| warning: false
#| message: false

selected_heatmap_audit <- list()

if (!selected_expression_ready) {
  selected_heatmap_audit[[1]] <- tibble::tibble(
    method_name = selected_de_method,
    plot_family = "Selected-contrast heatmap",
    expression_object = selected_expression_object_name,
    status = "skipped",
    reason = selected_expression_reason,
    genes_plotted = NA_integer_,
    samples_plotted = NA_integer_,
    output_file = NA_character_,
    contrast = NA_character_,
    segment_filter = ifelse(nzchar(params$selected_segment_regex), params$selected_segment_regex, "<all segments / infer from contrast>"),
    adj_p_cutoff = params$selected_heatmap_adj_p_cutoff,
    heatmap_gene_limit = params$heatmap_max_genes,
    heatmap_limit_parameter = "heatmap_max_genes"
  )
  emit_markdown(glue::glue("::: {{.callout-note}}\nSelected heatmaps were skipped. {selected_expression_reason}\n:::\n"))
} else {
  emit_markdown("\n\n### Selected heatmap contrast panels {.panel-tabset}\n\n")

  for (contrast_name in selected_contrasts) {
    heatmap_contrast_label <- selected_contrast_heatmap_label(contrast_name)
    emit_markdown(glue::glue("\n\n#### {heatmap_contrast_label}\n\n"))

    heatmap_result <- safe_plot_call(
      make_selected_contrast_heatmap(contrast_name, selected_results[[contrast_name]], selected_expression_object),
      glue::glue("Selected heatmap for {selected_de_method}/{contrast_name}")
    )

    display_visual_result(
      heatmap_result,
      "Focused selected-contrast heatmap",
      glue::glue("Focused row-scaled heatmap for {selected_de_method}, {heatmap_contrast_label}. The plotted genes pass the selected adjusted P-value cutoff and are limited by `heatmap_max_genes` ({params$heatmap_max_genes}). Sample annotations support review of whether the focused comparison is biologically coherent.")
    )

    selected_heatmap_audit[[contrast_name]] <- visual_result_to_audit(
      heatmap_result,
      selected_de_method,
      "Selected-contrast heatmap",
      selected_expression_object_name
    ) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        contrast = contrast_name,
        segment_filter = ifelse(nzchar(params$selected_segment_regex), params$selected_segment_regex, "<all segments / infer from contrast>"),
        adj_p_cutoff = params$selected_heatmap_adj_p_cutoff,
        heatmap_gene_limit = params$heatmap_max_genes,
        heatmap_limit_parameter = "heatmap_max_genes",
        contrast_heatmap_label = heatmap_contrast_label
      )
  }
}

selected_heatmap_audit_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(selected_heatmap_audit)
readr::write_csv(selected_heatmap_audit_df, fs::path(out_tables, "selected_contrast_heatmap_audit.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  selected_heatmap_audit_df,
  caption = "Selected-contrast heatmap configuration and render status",
  page_length = 10,
  scroll_y = "320px"
)
```

## Top DE genes by selected contrast

::: {.callout-note}
## What this section shows
For each selected contrast, the top `top_n_genes` significant genes (ranked by adjusted P-value, then absolute log fold-change) are drawn as per-gene boxplots so you can see where the contrast's strongest genes are expressed.

Each contrast shows an **All matched samples** panel — every sample assigned to the contrast's numerator and denominator arms, pooled across *all* segments. This is the contrast-level view of the top genes: it shows their expression across the cell-type groups the comparison is built from, using the whole matched sample set rather than any single segment.

When a distinct segment column is available, the panel also gains **one tab per segment** — the same top genes restricted to each tissue segment (for example the PanCK epithelial and PanCK-negative / stromal compartments), so you can check whether the contrast's signal is segment-specific. This per-segment strip is controlled by `boxplot_segment_tabs`: under the default `"auto"` it is shown only when the segment column is genuinely distinct from the contrast/grouping axis, and is suppressed automatically when segment is already built into your contrasts or there is no separate segment column (set `"always"` to force it on, `"never"` to force it off).

The x-axis is the `boxplot_x_col` metadata column (`cell_type` by default) — the cell-type groups the contrasts compare — and points are coloured by `boxplot_color_col` (`disease_status` by default), so the recovered Tumour/Healthy split is visible within each cell type. Stage 5 contrast metadata restricts the plotted samples to the numerator and denominator arms; it does not set the visual grouping. A panel is still rendered when only one x-axis group is present after filtering, in which case the boxes summarise the top genes within that single group. For one-vs-rest contrasts, the denominator arm aggregates the denominator terms recorded in `contrast_table_expanded.tsv`.
:::

```{r}
#| label: selected-top-gene-boxplots
#| results: asis
#| warning: false

plot_summaries <- list()
missing_gene_summaries <- list()

if (!selected_expression_ready) {
  plot_summaries[[1]] <- make_plot_summary(
    source_label = "top_genes",
    plot_family = "top_genes",
    contrast_name = "",
    scope = "segment_filtered",
    file_stub = "top_genes_skipped",
    requested_genes = 0L,
    status = "skipped",
    reason = selected_expression_reason
  )
  emit_markdown(glue::glue("::: {{.callout-note}}\nTop-gene boxplots were skipped. {selected_expression_reason}\n:::\n"))
} else {
  emit_markdown("\n\n### Top DE contrast panels {.panel-tabset}\n\n")

  for (contrast_name in selected_contrasts) {
    top_gene_contrast_label <- selected_contrast_heatmap_label(contrast_name)
    emit_markdown(glue::glue("\n\n#### {top_gene_contrast_label}\n\n"))

    clean_tbl <- standardise_de_table(selected_results[[contrast_name]])
    if (is.null(clean_tbl) || nrow(clean_tbl) == 0) {
      plot_summaries[[length(plot_summaries) + 1]] <- make_plot_summary(
        source_label = glue::glue("top_genes:{contrast_name}"),
        plot_family = "top_genes",
        contrast_name = contrast_name,
        scope = "segment_filtered",
        file_stub = sanitize_file_name(glue::glue("top_genes_{selected_de_method}_{contrast_name}_skipped")),
        requested_genes = 0L,
        contrast_label = top_gene_contrast_label,
        status = "skipped_empty_result_table",
        reason = "Selected contrast table was empty or invalid."
      )
      next
    }

    top_genes <- clean_tbl %>%
      dplyr::filter(
        is.finite(.data$adj.P.Val),
        is.finite(.data$logFC),
        .data$adj.P.Val < params$adj_p_cutoff,
        abs(.data$logFC) > params$logFC_cutoff
      ) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(.data$adj.P.Val, dplyr::desc(abs(.data$logFC))) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = params$top_n_genes) %>%
      dplyr::pull(.data$gene)

    if (length(top_genes) == 0) {
      plot_summaries[[length(plot_summaries) + 1]] <- make_plot_summary(
        source_label = glue::glue("top_genes:{contrast_name}"),
        plot_family = "top_genes",
        contrast_name = contrast_name,
        scope = "segment_filtered",
        file_stub = sanitize_file_name(glue::glue("top_genes_{selected_de_method}_{contrast_name}_skipped")),
        requested_genes = 0L,
        contrast_label = top_gene_contrast_label,
        status = "skipped_no_significant_genes",
        reason = "No genes passed the selected adjusted P-value and logFC thresholds for this contrast."
      )
      next
    }

    result <- plot_top_gene_segment_panels(genes = top_genes, contrast_name = contrast_name)

    plot_summaries <- c(plot_summaries, result$summaries)
    missing_gene_summaries <- c(missing_gene_summaries, result$missing)
  }
}
```

## Plot checklist tables

These checklist tables record what selected-method figures were attempted, what was plotted, and why any requested panel was skipped.

```{r}
#| label: selected-plot-audit-tables
#| results: asis
#| warning: false

plot_summary_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(plot_summaries)
missing_genes_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(missing_gene_summaries)

if (nrow(plot_summary_df) == 0) {
  plot_summary_df <- tibble::tibble(
    source = character(),
    plot_family = character(),
    contrast = character(),
    scope = character(),
    figure = character(),
    requested_genes = integer(),
    plotted_genes = integer(),
    missing_genes = integer(),
    segment_filter = character(),
    segment_label = character(),
    contrast_label = character(),
    disease_status_levels = character(),
    boxplot_x_column = character(),
    boxplot_color_column = character(),
    boxplot_facet_column = character(),
    samples_plotted = integer(),
    output_pdf = character(),
    output_png = character(),
    status = character(),
    reason = character()
  )
}

if (nrow(missing_genes_df) == 0) {
  missing_genes_df <- tibble::tibble(
    source = character(),
    plot_family = character(),
    contrast = character(),
    scope = character(),
    figure = character(),
    gene = character(),
    reason = character()
  )
}

readr::write_csv(plot_summary_df, fs::path(out_tables, "gene_boxplot_summary.csv"))
readr::write_csv(missing_genes_df, fs::path(out_tables, "missing_requested_genes.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  plot_summary_df,
  caption = "Selected-method gene-level plot checklist summary",
  page_length = 15,
  scroll_y = "420px"
)

protocol_datatable(
  missing_genes_df,
  caption = "Requested genes not plotted and reason",
  page_length = 15,
  scroll_y = "300px"
)
```

::: {.callout-important}
## Decision checkpoint before pathway enrichment
Use the method inventories, volcano plots, heatmaps, overlap summaries, and selected-method gene-level plots above to choose the method and contrasts to carry into `7_functional_enrichment.qmd`. Record the selected method, selected contrasts, segment filter, and any caveats in your run notes before continuing.
:::

## Output list

```{r}
#| label: output-list

output_list_files <- c(
  list.files(out_results_de, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE),
  list.files(out_tables, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE),
  list.files(out_figures_de, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
)

phase6_output_list <- tibble::tibble(path = unique(output_list_files)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(file.exists(.data$path)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(relative_path = fs::path_rel(.data$path, start = project_root)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(.data$relative_path)

readr::write_csv(phase6_output_list, fs::path(out_tables, "phase6_output_list.csv"))

protocol_datatable(
  phase6_output_list,
  caption = "Stage 6 output list",
  page_length = 15,
  scroll_y = "420px"
)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Session Info

```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
:::
